Entries.preload.telemetry.title: Privacy
Entries.preload.telemetry.description:
To improve the game, we would like to collect anonymized statistics about balancing, playtime, level completion, crashes, performance, system specs, settings, and achievements.

This involves a third-party service called "Unity Analytics". All data is sent anonymously and cannot be traced back to you.

If you'd like to know more, check our <link:privacy-policy>Privacy Policy</link>. You can also opt out below if you're not comfortable with this.

Entries.research.Milestone_InitialOnboarding.des:
Alright, here's your starter kit - everything you need to <gl>Extract</gl>, <gl>Cut</gl> and <gl>Rotate</gl> shapes.

<info>Try not to break anything on day one.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_OnboardingStacker.des: Your performance so far is... acceptable. Here's the Stacker - it lets you stack shapes on top of each other. You'll need Belt Lifts to use it. Let's see what you can do with it.
You can now <gl>Copy & Paste</gl> parts of your factory. Save designs, reuse them, scale up faster.

<info>... but not the messy ones, please.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_OnboardingSpaceBuildi:
After careful observation, we're allowing you to expand beyond the <gl>Vortex Platform</gl> and build <gl>Space Platforms</gl>.

<info>... there's a limit though. We're not crazy.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_OnboardingBadge.title: Operator License
Entries.research.Milestone_OnboardingBadge.descr:
Oh, you actually made it this far? <info>Genuinely didn't expect that.</info>

Well then - claim your <gl>Operator License</gl> and get out there. Real factories are waiting, and the <gl>Vortex</gl> must grow.
Entries.research.Milestone_InitialClassic.title: Certification
Entries.research.Milestone_InitialClassic.descri:
You've got the <gl>Operator License</gl>, so we've set you up with a basic toolkit. Extractors, cutters, rotators - the essentials.

<info>The rest? You'll have to earn it.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_FluidPainting.title: Fluids & Painting
Entries.research.Milestone_FluidPainting.descrip:
You get the drill by now. Actually - a real drill. Here's your <gl>Mini Drill Pump</gl>. Extract <gl>Fluids</gl> and <gl>Paint</gl> your shapes any color you want.

<info>... do us a favor and don't spill any on the floor. They stick.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_ShapeTrains.title: Trains
Entries.research.Milestone_ShapeTrains.descripti:
We know you've been waiting for these. Move <gl>Huge Amounts</gl> of shapes over <gl>Long Distances</gl> - the dream of every operator.

<info>Choo choo.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_PinPusher.title: Pin Pusher
Entries.research.Milestone_PinPusher.description:
This one's fun - it <gl>Pushes</gl> a shape up and creates a <gl>Support Pin</gl> below every non-empty quarter on the first layer.

<info>Floating shapes are dead. Long live floating shapes.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_ColorMixing.title: Color Mixing
Entries.research.Milestone_ColorMixing.descripti:
Time to get creative. The <gl>Color Mixer</gl> lets you combine any two colors to create <gl>Secondary</gl> and <gl>Tertiary</gl> colors.

<info>The possibilities are... well, exactly 9. But still.</info>
Entries.research.Milestone_PlatformLayer3.title: 3rd Space Level
Entries.research.Milestone_PlatformLayer3.descri: More vertical space for your operations. You can now build on a 3rd level in space. Remember - no spaghetti factories. You signed the contract.
Entries.research.Milestone_Crystals.title: Crystals
Entries.research.Milestone_Crystals.description:
Let's spice things up with <gl>Crystals</gl>. Fill fluid into empty <gl>Gaps</gl> or <gl>Support Pins</gl> of a shape and harden it with the <gl>Crystal Generator</gl>.

Fair warning - they're a little fragile. Don't <gl>Cut</gl> through them or <gl>Stack</gl> on top.

<info>FRAGILE - DO NOT DROP, BREAK OR EAT.</info>
Entries.research.MSFinal_Classic.title: Final Qualification
Entries.research.MSFinal_Classic.description:
Well, well. You actually <gl>Passed</gl> the final qualification. <info>Impressive.</info>

Keep pushing your <gl>Operator Level</gl> to compete against other operators - or try <gl>Manufacture Mode</gl> if you're ready for something different.
Entries.research.MSFinal_ClassicConverters.title: Full Clearance
Entries.research.MSFinal_ClassicConverters.descr:
You've completed every milestone. <info>Not bad, Operator.</info>

You now have access to <gl>Trade Stations</gl> - exchange your shapes for increasingly exotic refined shapes. On top of that, your <gl>Operator Shape Line</gl> goals are now randomized and infinite.

Time to see how far you can push this factory.
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier0.title: Beyond the Vortex
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier0.descr:
You're still here? Most operators call it a day after the final qualification.

<info>Not you though. Noted.</info>
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier1.title: Deep Operations
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier1.descr:
The shapes are getting more complex and the targets steeper. This is uncharted territory - even for us.

<info>We're genuinely curious how far you'll go.</info>
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier2.title: Expert Operator
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier2.descr:
At this point you probably know more about shape operations than most of the engineering team.

<info>Don't tell them we said that.</info>
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier3.title: Vortex Singularity
Entries.research.MSPostFinal_Classic_Tier3.descr:
The <gl>Vortex</gl> is operating at levels we didn't think were possible. Whatever you're doing - keep doing it.

<info>Management is watching. In a good way. Probably.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTierInitial.d:
Alright, Operator. I've arranged for you to receive everything you need to start processing waste. Connect to the <gl>Waste Provider</gl> and deliver shapes to the <gl>Polishing Station</gl>. This station polishes damaged shapes into usable ones.

<info>Welcome to waste management. It's not glamorous, but it pays.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier1.title: Ruby & Trains
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier1.descrip:
Polished waste is a start, but the traders want more. Process it further at the <gl>Ruby Station</gl> to produce genuine <gl>Ruby</gl>. This also unlocks <gl>Trains</gl> - you'll need them to move shapes across your operation efficiently.

<info>From garbage to gemstones. What a career.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier2.title: Sapphire & Painting
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier2.descrip:
Ruby opens doors, but <gl>Sapphire</gl> opens better ones. The traders have agreed to accept it. You'll also gain access to <gl>Painters</gl> - valuable for preparing the precise refined shapes these stations require.

The traders are starting to take you seriously.

<info>Almost.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier3.title: Emerald
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier3.descrip:
Sapphire traders have connected you with their <gl>Emerald</gl> contacts. These stations are larger and demand more complex inputs. The outputs, however, are considerably more valuable.

Progress is progress.

<info>We're watching.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier4.title: Gold
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier4.descrip:
<gl>Gold</gl>. Real gold. The traders out here don't ask where it comes from, and neither should you. Just deliver the right shapes and collect your share.

Your Vortex fund is growing.

<info>Don't get attached to the gold. It's not for keeping.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier5.title: Aquamarine & Mixing
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier5.descrip:
The <gl>Aquamarine Station</gl> requires precisely mixed colors. You'll need the <gl>Color Mixer</gl> to create the refined shapes they accept.

Complex work, but that's why they pay in aquamarine.

<info>The traders respect precision. So should you.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier6.title: Amethyst & 3rd Space Level
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier6.descrip:
<gl>Amethyst</gl> processing demands vertical expansion. The <gl>3rd Space Level</gl> is now available - use it wisely.

These stations are demanding.

<info>But then again, so is rebuilding an entire Vortex platform from scratch.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier7.title: Diamonds & Support Pins
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier7.descrip:
<gl>Diamonds</gl>. The traders saved these contacts for operators who prove themselves. The <gl>Diamond Station</gl> requires shapes with <gl>Support Pins</gl> - intricate work, but the payout reflects it.

<info>You're getting closer. The Grand-Assembler is almost within reach.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier8.title: Painite & Crystals
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier8.descrip:
<gl>Painite</gl> - one of the rarest refined shapes known. The station requires <gl>Crystal</gl> refined shapes, created by refining fluids into solid crystalline shapes.

This is the final tier before construction begins.

<info>Make it count.</info>

Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier9.title: Vortex Grand-Assembler
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTier9.descrip:
You've accumulated enough shapes. The <gl>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gl> is now accessible. This massive station will construct your new Vortex platform piece by piece.

Deliver the required shapes. All of them.

<info>No shortcuts.</info>
Entries.research.ConverterMilestoneTierFinal.des: The Grand-Assembler has completed construction. Your new Vortex platform is ready for launch. We will consider reinstating your factory clearance. As promised. No promises about the outcome.

Entries.research.CBSpecial_RandomizedGoals.title: Infinite Goals
Entries.research.CBSpecial_RandomizedGoals.descr: Unlocks randomized operator level goals to challenge yourself & earn rewards.
Entries.research.CBSpecial_SpaceBuilding.title: Space Platforms
Entries.research.CBSpecial_SpaceBuilding.descrip: Expand your factory by building platforms in space.
Entries.research.CBSpecial_SpaceFloor3.title: 3rd Space Level
Entries.research.CBSpecial_SpaceFloor3.descripti: Unlocks an additional <gl>Space Level</gl>, enabling you to build even more compact factories.
Entries.research.CBStacking_BentStacker.title: <copy-from:building-variant.StackerDefaultVariant.title/>
Entries.research.CBStacking_Stacker.title: <copy-from:building-variant.StackerStraightVariant.title/>
Entries.research.CBTrains_Core.title: Trains
Entries.research.CBTrains_Core.description: Unlocks <gl>Trains</gl> for high-throughput transport of shapes and fluids over large distances.
Entries.research.CBTrains_EmptyTransport.title: Spacer Wagons
Entries.research.CBTrains_EmptyTransport.descrip: Unlocks <gl>Spacer Wagons</gl>, which can be used to space out other wagons.
Entries.research.CBTrains_FluidTransport.title: Fluid Wagons
Entries.research.CBTrains_FluidTransport.descrip: Unlocks <gl>Fluid Wagons, Loaders & Unloaders</gl>, allowing you to transport fluids via trains.
Entries.research.CBTrains_LinePackPrimary.title: Primary Colors
Entries.research.CBTrains_LinePackPrimary.descri: Unlocks <gl>Green</gl> and <gl>Blue</gl> trains for more routing options.
Entries.research.CBTrains_LinePackSecondary.desc: Unlocks <gl>Cyan</gl>, <gl>Magenta</gl> and <gl>Yellow</gl> trains for more routing options.
Entries.research.CBTrains_LineRed.title: Red Color
Entries.research.CBTrains_LineRed.description: Unlocks <gl>Red</gl> trains for more routing options.
Entries.research.CBTrains_LineWhite.title: White Color
Entries.research.CBTrains_LineWhite.description: Unlocks <gl>White</gl> trains for more routing options.
Entries.research.CBTrains_ShapeTransport.title: Shape Transport
Entries.research.CBTrains_ShapeTransport.descrip: Unlocks <gl>Shape Wagons, Loaders & Unloaders</gl>, allowing you to transport shapes via trains.
Entries.research.CBTrains_StationSpacer.title: Station Spacer
Entries.research.CBTrains_StationSpacer.descript: Unlocks Station Spacers, for building Stops with gaps between Loaders or Unloaders.
Entries.research.CBTrains_TrainStopHalting.title: Wait Stop
Entries.research.CBTrains_TrainStopHalting.descr: Unlocks a new <gl>Train Stop</gl>, where trains will always stop and wait until fully loaded or unloaded - up to 60 seconds.
Entries.research.CBTrains_TrainStopQuick.title: Immediate Stop
Entries.research.CBTrains_TrainStopQuick.descrip: Unlocks a new Train Stop, where trains only stop if they can immediately load/unload cargo, otherwise the stop is skipped.
Entries.research.CBTrains_TransferStations.title: Wagon Transfer Platform
Entries.research.CBTrains_TransferStations.descr: Unlocks <gl>Wagon Transfer Platforms</gl>, allowing you to quickly exchange packages between wagons.
Entries.research.CBTrains_VortexDelivery.title: Train Vortex Delivery
Entries.research.CBTrains_VortexDelivery.descrip:
<gl>Launch Trains</gl> directly into the <gl>Vortex</gl> to deliver shapes!

<info>The driver is fine. Don't worry about it.</info>
Entries.research.DLCBTrains_RailRollerCoaster.de: Unlocks the purely cosmetic <gl>Rail Loop</gl> and <gl>Rail Twister</gl>. Exclusive to the <gl>Supporter Edition</gl>.
Entries.research.CBWires_Core.title: Wire Basics
Entries.research.CBWires_GoalReceiver.title: Operator Shape Receiver
Entries.research.CBWires_GoalReceiver.descriptio: Unlocks the <gl>Operator Shape Receiver</gl>, allowing you to receive signals for all <gl>Operator Shapes</gl>. Requires 3rd Level.
Entries.research.CBWires_LogicGates.title: Logic Gates
Entries.research.CBWires_LogicGates.description: Unlocks logic gates to perform boolean logic with signals.
Entries.research.CBWires_UniversalTransmission.d: Unlocks <gl>Global Wire Transmitters</gl>, allowing you to transmit signals globally over different channels. Requires 3rd Level.
Entries.research.CBWires_VirtualProcessing.title: Simulated Machines
Entries.research.RNHubSize.title: More Vortex Inputs
Entries.research.RNHubSize.description: Increases the <gl>Size</gl> of the <gl>Vortex</gl> to allow for more inputs.
Entries.research.LRUGlobalSpeed.title: Speed Multiplier
Entries.research.LRUGlobalSpeed.description: Increases the speed of all Machines & Platforms - affects both processing & transport speed.
Entries.research.LRUTrainSpeed.title: Train Speed
Entries.research.LRUTrainSpeed.description: Increases the speed of <gl>Trains</gl>, decreasing turnaround times and requiring fewer trains to transport the same amount.
Entries.research.LRUTrainCapacity.title: Train Capacity
Entries.research.LRUTrainCapacity.description: Increases the amount of Packages that a Train Wagon can carry per Machine Level.
Entries.research.LRUHubInputSize.title: Vortex Inputs
Entries.research.LRUHubInputSize.description: Increases the number of conveyor belts you can feed into the <gl>Vortex</gl>.
Entries.research.LRUShapeQuantity.title: Shape Multiplier
Entries.research.LRUShapeQuantity.description: Makes every shape that you deliver to the <gl>Vortex</gl> count multiple times!
Entries.research.LRUChunkLimitAdd.title: Platform Capacity
Entries.research.LRUChunkLimitAdd.description: Increases the <gl><icon:ChunkLimit/> Platform Capacity</gl> available for building <gl>Space Platforms</gl>.
Entries.research.RUSandbox.title: Sandbox Tools
Entries.research.RUSandbox.description: Produce any shape or fluid on demand.
Entries.research.RUSideUpgrades.title: Shop
Entries.research.RUSideUpgrades.description: Unlocks the <gl>Shop Screen</gl>, allowing you to unlock advanced technologies.
Entries.research.RULayer2.title: 2nd Level
Entries.research.RULayer2.description: Unlocks the <gl>2nd Level</gl>, enabling you to build multidimensional factories.
Entries.research.RULayer3.title: 3rd Level
Entries.research.RULayer3.description: Unlocks the <gl>3rd Level</gl>, enabling you to build even more compact factories.
Entries.research.RUBlueprints.title: Blueprints
Entries.research.RUBlueprints.description: Copy and paste parts of your factory.
Entries.research.RUIslandPlacement.title: Space Platforms
Entries.research.RUIslandPlacement.description: Expand your factory by building platforms in space.
Entries.research.RUPlayerLevel.title: Operator Level
Entries.research.RUPlayerLevel.description: Keep delivering past milestone shapes to increase your <gl>Operator Level</gl>, granting you rewards from the <gl>Vortex</gl>.
Entries.research.RUTrains.title: Trains
Entries.research.RUTrains.description: Transport large amounts of shapes and fluids over long distances.
Entries.research.RUFluids.title: Fluids
Entries.research.RUFluids.description: Extract, transport, and mix fluids to paint shapes.
Entries.research.RUWires.title: Wires
Entries.research.RUWires.description: Dynamically control your factories with wires and logic signals.
Entries.research.RUTrainHubDelivery.title: Train Vortex Delivery
Entries.research.RUTrainHubDelivery.description: <gl>Launch Trains</gl> directly into the <gl>Vortex</gl> to deliver shapes!

Entries.research.RUInfiniteGoals.description: Unlocks infinite, randomly generated operator level goals to challenge yourself and earn rewards.
Entries.research.RUOperatorBadge.title: Operator License
Entries.research.RUOperatorBadge.description: The <gl>Operator License</gl> certifies you as a qualified factory operator.
Entries.research.RUTradeStations.title: Trade Stations
Entries.research.RUTradeStations.description: Unlocks Trade Stations for exchanging shapes.
Entries.research.RUClassicTier1CMY.title: CMY Trade Stations
Entries.research.RUClassicTier1CMY.description: Unlocks CMY Trade Stations for combining refined shapes.
Entries.research.RUClassicTier2WK.title: WK Trade Stations
Entries.research.RUClassicTier2WK.description: Unlocks White and Black Trade Stations for advanced shape trading.
Entries.research.RUClassicTier3VGA.title: Vortex Grand-Assembler
Entries.research.RUClassicTier3VGA.description: Unlocks the Vortex Grand-Assembler for the final trade.
Entries.research.RUPinPushing.title: Pin Pushing
Entries.research.RUPinPushing.description: Unlocks the Pin Pusher for pushing pins onto shapes.
Entries.research.RUColorMixing.title: Color Mixing
Entries.research.RUColorMixing.description: Unlocks the Mixer for combining fluids into new colors.
Entries.research.RUCrystals.title: Crystals
Entries.research.RUCrystals.description: Unlocks the Crystal Generator for crystallizing fluids into shapes.

Entries.island-layout.Layout_RailPath_Blue.descr: Drag over a <gl>Rail Intersection</gl> to change which direction <gl>Blue</gl> trains will take.

Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainTransfer_Shape: Transfers <gl>Shape Packages</gl> directly between <gl>Shape Wagons</gl> without unpacking.
Loads fluids from Space Pipes onto Fluid Wagons as compressed Fluid Packages.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainTransfer_Fluid: Transfers <gl>Fluid Packages</gl> directly between <gl>Fluid Wagons</gl> without unpacking.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainSpacerStraight: Train Stations must be contiguous. Place spacers to create gaps between station modules.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainQuickStation.d: Trains will only stop here if they can be immediately loaded or unloaded, otherwise the stop is skipped.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainWaitStation.de: Trains will stop and wait here up to 60 seconds or until fully loaded/unloaded.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainShapeWagonExte: Place behind a <gl>Locomotive Depot</gl> to add a <gl>Shape Wagon</gl> to the train.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainFluidWagonExte: Place behind a <gl>Locomotive Depot</gl> to add a <gl>Fluid Wagon</gl> to the train.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainFillerWagonExt: Place behind a <gl>Locomotive Depot</gl> to add a <gl>Spacer Wagon</gl> to the train.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainProducer_Red.d: Provides one <gl>Locomotive</gl>. Add <gl>Wagon Depots</gl> to expand the train.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainTwister.title: Rail Twister
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainTwister.descri:
Decorative only, makes trains <gl>Twist</gl> 360 degrees.

Part of the <gl>Supporter Edition</gl>.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_TrainRollerCoasterL:
Decorative only, makes trains move in a <gl>Loop</gl>.

Part of the <gl>Supporter Edition</gl>.
Entries.research.RUTier1Ruby.title: Ruby
Entries.research.RUTier1Ruby.description: Unlocks the <gl>Ruby Station</gl>. Converts polished waste into genuine Ruby - a shape the traders actually value.
Entries.research.RUTier2Sapphire.title: Sapphire
Entries.research.RUTier2Sapphire.description: Unlocks the <gl>Sapphire Station</gl>. Refines Ruby into Sapphire through thermal recombination. Requires painted shape inputs.
Entries.research.RUTier3Emerald.title: Emerald
Entries.research.RUTier3Emerald.description: Unlocks the <gl>Emerald Station</gl>. Larger station footprint, multiple inputs required. The traders pay well for this one.
Entries.research.RUTier4Gold.title: Gold
Entries.research.RUTier4Gold.description: Unlocks the <gl>Gold Station</gl>. Produces actual gold. Four separate outputs - handle the logistics accordingly.
Entries.research.RUTier5Aquamarine.title: Aquamarine
Entries.research.RUTier5Aquamarine.description: Unlocks the <gl>Aquamarine Station</gl>. Requires mixed-color inputs. The station is demanding, but worth it.
Entries.research.RUTier6Amethyst.title: Amethyst
Entries.research.RUTier6Amethyst.description: Unlocks the <gl>Amethyst Station</gl>. Complex layered inputs required. Available only with 3rd Space Level access.
Entries.research.RUTier7Diamonds.title: Diamonds
Entries.research.RUTier7Diamonds.description: Unlocks the <gl>Diamond Station</gl>. Requires shapes with support pins. The most valuable crystalline shape before Painite.
Entries.research.RUTier8Painite.title: Painite
Entries.research.RUTier8Painite.description: Unlocks the <gl>Painite Station</gl>. Requires crystal refined shapes. One of the rarest shapes - essential for Vortex construction.
Entries.research.RUTier9VortexTrader.title: Vortex Construction
Entries.research.RUTier9VortexTrader.description: Unlocks the <gl>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gl>. This massive station constructs your new Vortex platform from accumulated shapes.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_DonationStation.des: Donate refined shapes to increase your <gl>Operator Level</gl> and receive <gl><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gl>.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_ResearchStation.des: Deliver Refined Shapes to increase your Operator Level and receive a byproduct as output.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier1_B.d: Get better trade conditions on <gl>Ruby</gl> in exchange for more complicated inputs.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier2_B.d: Get better trade conditions on <gl>Sapphire</gl> in exchange for more complicated inputs.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier3_B.d: Build your custom connector platform to utilize this more efficient <gl>Emerald</gl> trading station.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier4_B.d: Get better trade conditions on <gl>Gold</gl> in exchange for more complicated inputs.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier5_B.d: Get better trade conditions on <gl>Aquamarine</gl> in exchange for more complicated inputs.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_Converter_Tier6_B.d: Get better trade conditions on <gl>Amethyst</gl>, requires creative <gl>Space Belt</gl> routing.
Entries.island-layout.Layout_ConverterHub.title: Waste Provider
Entries.island-layout.Layout_ConverterHub.descri: Provides thermally processed <gl>Waste</gl> from the other side of the simulation. Your raw material supply.

Entries.wiki.WKMisc_GameModes.title: Game Modes
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_GameModes.text-intro-1: shapez 2 features three <gl>Game Modes</gl>, each offering a different experience. You can select the mode when starting a new savegame.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_GameModes.text-certification:
The <gl>Certification</gl> is a guided tutorial that teaches you the basics of factory building. Vortex LTD requires every operator to complete the program and earn their <gl>Operator License</gl> before being allowed to work in real factories.

Recommended for all new operators.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_GameModes.text-classic:
<gl>Classic Mode</gl> is the standard shapez 2 experience - a well-balanced blend of factory building and shape puzzles. Deliver shapes to the <gl>Vortex</gl> to complete <gll:WKResearch_Milestones>Milestones</gll>, unlock new <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll> and progress through the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>.

Recommended as the first mode for new players after completing the Certification.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_GameModes.text-manufacture-c:
<gl>Manufacture Mode</gl> focuses on building large-scale, permanent factories. Process dimensional waste through a chain of <gl>Trade Stations</gl> to collect refined shapes and rebuild your <gl>Vortex Platform</gl> at the <gl>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gl>.

Unlike Classic Mode, your factories stay relevant throughout the entire game - plan ahead and build for scale.

Recommended after advancing in Classic Mode and getting comfortable with <gll:WKIslands_ModularBuilds>Modular Platform Setups</gll>.

<gl>Manufacture Mode</gl> focuses on building large-scale, permanent factories. Process dimensional waste through a chain of <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll> to collect refined shapes and rebuild your <gl>Vortex Platform</gl> at the <gll:WKTradeStations_VortexGrandAssembler>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gll>.

Progress through 9 tiers of increasingly valuable shapes and unlock new stations and <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll> along the way. Unlike Classic Mode, your factories stay relevant throughout the entire game - plan ahead and build for scale.

Recommended after advancing in Classic Mode and getting comfortable with <gll:WKIslands_ModularBuilds>Modular Platform Setups</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKTutorialWelcome.title: Certification Program
Entries.wiki.WKTutorialWelcome.text-intro-1: Welcome to the Operator Certification program! You did register, right...? Before Vortex LTD lets you anywhere near a real factory, we need to make sure you know what you're doing. Regulations. You understand. You have one job - deliver the requested shapes as efficiently as possible. Prove your skills and you'll earn your Operator License along with access to new Technologies.
Entries.wiki.WKTutorialWelcome.text-intro-2: We've provided you with an unlimited amount of buildings to get the job done. Try not to break too many - we still need them for the next participant. Good luck out there.
Entries.wiki.WKConverterWelcome.title: Incident Recovery Protocol
Entries.wiki.WKConverterWelcome.text-intro-1: Welcome back, Operator. Let's address it directly: your Vortex overheated and imploded. The facility is gone, the platform is gone, and your operational clearance has been - let's call it suspended. We cannot let you near a real factory right now. Regulations. You understand. However, we are not unreasonable. We have a new job available for you on the other side of the simulation, where the shapes that other operators deliver into their Vortexes end up as thermally processed Waste. Someone has to deal with that. Take the damaged shapes, polish them, and bring them to the Trade Stations to exchange them for better shapes - emerald, gold, diamonds.
Entries.wiki.WKConverterWelcome.text-intro-2:
Collect enough and you can begin assembling a new Vortex platform at the <gl>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gl>.

Complete it, and we will <info>consider</info> reinstating your factory clearance. No promises.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicRegularWelcome.title: Factory Assignment
Entries.wiki.WKClassicRegularWelcome.text-intro-: Welcome, fresh Operator! Congratulations again on earning your Operator License - not everyone makes it through the Operator Certification program, you know. Vortex LTD really needs you here - the demand for shapes has never been higher and we're running behind schedule.

Your task is straightforward: deliver the requested shapes to the Vortex as efficiently as possible. Prove yourself and you'll unlock access to new Technologies and expand your operation. This is a standard simulation - nothing too crazy. Build smart, scale up, and you'll do just fine.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHardWelcome.title: Advanced Simulation
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHardWelcome.text-intro-1:
You've got the fundamentals down. Now it's time to put them to the test. <info>Good call.</info>

This simulation is a little more difficult than the standard one, but definitely doable. The shapes are more complex, the requirements tighter, and you'll need to think a few steps ahead.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHardWelcome.text-intro-2: Don't be discouraged - it's nothing an experienced operator like you can't handle. Plan carefully and keep your factory organized. Best of luck, Operator.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicInsaneWelcome.title: Unstable Simulation Warning
Entries.wiki.WKClassicInsaneWelcome.text-intro-1: WARNING - Last chance to step back, Operator. This simulation has been quite unstable and only the most experienced operators will be able to deal with the conditions here. The shapes are absurdly complex, the targets unforgiving, and mistakes are very costly. Seriously - this is your last chance to go back and pick a different assignment.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicInsaneWelcome.text-intro-2:
Actually, you did already sign up? <info>Ah well...</info>

Good luck in there. You're going to need it.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHexWelcome.title: Emergency Replacement Simulation
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHexWelcome.text-intro-1: There's no easy way to explain this - the last simulation kind of... collapsed. Good news - Vortex LTD formed a strike team to quickly put something new together. Bad news - they had an intern working on it, and somehow they made a "slight" error. Instead of the usual 4 sides, shapes now have 6. We tried to fix it but the changes are... permanent. Management says it's fine. Perfectly fine. Probably.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicHexWelcome.text-intro-2:
Don't worry - everything works exactly as before. <info>As I've been told.</info>

Good luck, Operator.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_Overview.title: Trade Stations
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_Overview.text-intro:
<gl>Trade Stations</gl> let you trade shapes for more valuable ones. Each station has a single recipe - deliver the required input via <gll:WKIslands_SpaceBelts>Space Belts</gll> and collect the upgraded output on the other side.

Higher-tier stations require more complex inputs but yield more valuable <gl>Refined Shapes</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_Overview.text-robot: The rockets handle everything - packaging, dimensional transit, customs. Yes, there are interdimensional customs. No, you don't want to see the paperwork.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_Overview.text-intro: Trade Stations process shapes on a per-lane basis. Each Space Belt carries 12 lanes - 4 per Machine Level. If a station requires multiple inputs, the shapes at each input must arrive on the same lane to be matched - the output will also appear on that lane.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ConverterHub.title: Waste Provider
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ConverterHub.text-i: The central structure that receives dimensional <gl>Waste</gl> and produces shapes on its Space Belts.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ConverterHub.text-d: The Waste Provider is the starting point of every factory in Manufacture Mode. It receives dimensional waste and outputs it as shapes on Space Belts at maximum speed. Process these shapes - cut, rotate, stack, and paint them - then deliver the results to Trade Stations in exchange for refined shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ConverterHub.text-r: In Classic, these shapes go straight to the trash. In Manufacture, they're the whole business plan.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ManufactureMode.tex:
Alright, Operator. You remember the incident. Your <gl>Vortex</gl> imploded, your clearance is suspended, and now you're on waste duty.

The plan: process <gll:WKTradeStations_ConverterHub>Waste</gll> at <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll> to collect refined shapes, then rebuild your platform at the <gll:WKTradeStations_VortexGrandAssembler>Vortex Grand-Assembler</gll>.

Stations only produce output once their <gll:WKResearch_Milestones>Milestone</gll> is completed. Until then, shapes go in but nothing comes back.

There are 9 tiers of stations, each producing more valuable shapes - Ruby, Sapphire, Emerald, Gold, Aquamarine, Amethyst, Diamond, and Painite. Each tier unlocks new stations and Machines. Some tiers have Alternate Trade Stations in the Shop with different recipes. Check the Trades Screen for the full list.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_DonationStation.tex:
The <gl>Donation Station</gl> lets you sacrifice shapes to increase your <gll:WKResearch_OperatorLevel>Operator Level</gll> and earn <gll:WKResearch_ResearchPoints><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gll>. The required shapes are shown in the <gll:WKResearch_OperatorLevel>Operator Level Screen</gll>.

Higher levels unlock more advanced <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll> and progression rewards.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_ResearchStation.tex: The Research Station converts Refined Shapes into byproducts. Deliver the refined shapes listed in the Operator Level Screen and collect the output. These byproducts are required as inputs for higher-tier Trade Stations.
Entries.wiki.WKTradeStations_VortexGrandAssemble: The Vortex Grand-Assembler (VGA) is the final station in Manufacture Mode. This massive structure constructs your new Vortex platform piece by piece from the refined shapes you have collected.
The final destination for all your refined shapes.
The final step. No pressure. Well, some pressure. A lot of pressure, actually.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_RGB.title: RGB Trade Stations
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_RGB.text-int: The first tier of Trade Stations in Classic Mode. Each station takes a single input and produces one Refined Shape - available in Red, Green, and Blue variants. Deliver the required shape via Space Belts and collect the refined output on the other side. These outputs serve as inputs for the next tier of stations.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_CMY.title: CMY Trade Stations
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_CMY.text-int: The second tier of Trade Stations in Classic Mode. Each station takes two inputs and produces one Refined Shape - available in Cyan, Magenta, and Yellow variants. CMY stations combine outputs from RGB Trade Stations. Both inputs must arrive on the same lane to be matched.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_WK.title: WK Trade Stations
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_WK.text-intr: The third tier of Trade Stations in Classic Mode. Each station takes three inputs and produces one Refined Shape - available in White and Black variants. WK stations combine outputs from CMY Trade Stations. All three inputs must arrive on the same lane to be matched.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_VGA.title: Vortex Grand-Assembler
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_VGA.text-int: The final tier of Trade Stations in Classic Mode. The Vortex Grand-Assembler takes two inputs - one White and one Black refined shape - and produces Vortex Platforms. As a byproduct, it also produces new unique refined shapes that unlock the most efficient final Operator Shape recipes.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicTradeStations_VGA.text-rob: Every platform you assemble gets shipped to a new Operator somewhere. You're not just running a factory - you're keeping the whole system going.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Movement.title: Movement
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Movement.text-in: Use <hotkey:camera.move-up/>, <hotkey:camera.move-left/>, <hotkey:camera.move-down/>, <hotkey:camera.move-right/> to move the camera. You can also use <hotkey:camera.mouse-drag-modifier/>, but it does not work while placing structures.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Rotation.title: Camera Rotation
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Rotation.text-in: To rotate the camera, hold and move the mouse horizontally to change the direction, and vertically to change the perspective.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Zoom.title: Zoom
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Zoom.text-intro-: Zoom using your mouse wheel . Zooming automatically switches between Machine View and Space View.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_CameraSnapping.t: The camera automatically Snaps to multiples of 30 degrees by default. You can change this by clicking the angle icon in the bottom left corner of the screen. For example, with 90 degrees snapping the camera automatically snaps to either North, East, South or West.
You can switch levels with and . This works both in Machine View and Space View. Changing levels while placing Conveyor Belts, Space Belts, Space Pipes or Rails automatically creates vertical connections like Belt Lifts to transport items across levels.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_JumpToHub.title: Jump Home
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_JumpToHub.text-i: If you ever get lost, you can click the Home Icon in the upper right or press to jump back to the center of the map.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_Visualizations.t: Visualizations can be toggled in the lower right. There are different visualizations available in <gll:WKProcessing_BuildingMode>Machine View</gll> and <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_MapMarkers.title: Location Markers
Entries.wiki.WKCameraNavigation_MapMarkers.text-: Place Location Markers to find your factories in space again. You can either press or click the Add Marker button in the upper right. You can set a name, shape icon or both for a marker, making them easier to distinguish.
Shape icons are set by entering a Shape Code. Otherwise invalid shape codes are allowed here - for example, shapes with just 3 parts like CuCuCu work just fine.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Placement.title: Placement
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Rotation.title: Rotation
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Rotation.text-intro-1:
You can <gl>Rotate</gl> any structure or blueprint with <hotkey:building-placement.rotate-cw/> during placement.

Use <hotkey:building-placement.rotate-ccw/> to rotate counter-clockwise.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Deletion.title: Deletion
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Deletion.text-intro-1: Delete structures with - drag to delete multiple structures in one go. You can also use Area Deletion to delete entire areas at once.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Deletion.text-robot: We know you don't make mistakes, Operator. This is just in case you need to fix someone else's factory.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_UndoRedo.title: Undo / Redo
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_UndoRedo.text-intro-1: Undo actions with and Redo with . You can undo up to 100 actions.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_AreaSelection.title: Area Selection
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_AreaSelection.text-intr:
Select an area with <hotkey:mass-selection.select-area-modifier/> <hotkey:mass-selection.select-base/>, this works both in <gll:WKProcessing_BuildingMode>Machine View</gll> and <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll>.

Use <hotkey:mass-selection.deselect-area-modifier/> <hotkey:mass-selection.select-base/> to deselect parts of an area after you selected it.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_AreaDeletion.title: Area Deletion
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_AreaDeletion.text-intro: Drag with <hotkey:mass-selection.select-area-modifier/> + <hotkey:mass-selection.quick-delete-drag/> to delete an entire <gl>Area</gl> at once.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Toolbar.title: Toolbar
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Toolbar.text-intro-1: The Toolbar allows you to place structures. It automatically switches between machines and platforms when you enter Machine View or Space View. Use , , etc. to quickly select slots on the toolbar.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Mirroring.title: Mirroring
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Mirroring.text-intro-1:
All <gll:WKProcessing_BuildingMode>Machines</gll>, <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Platforms</gll> and <gll:WKBlueprints_Intro>Blueprints</gll> can be <gl>Mirrored</gl> with <hotkey:building-placement.mirror/>.

Use <hotkey:building-placement.mirror-inverse/> to mirror on the other axis.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Mirroring.text-robot: Everything can be mirrored. The factory, the machines, the blueprints. Everything except the Vortex. We tried. It didn't mirror. It looked back.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_SwitchingVariants.title: Switching Variants
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_SwitchingVariants.text-: Use to switch between different Machine Variants during placement, for example to switch between the different Rotators. You can also change the Toolbar Keybinding Repeat setting to Cycle Slots - this makes pressing the same toolbar keybinding again cycle through variants instead of deselecting.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_ClearContents.title: Clearing Contents
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_ClearContents.text-intr: Select an <gll:WKPlacement_AreaSelection>Area</gll> and press <hotkey:mass-selection.clear-selection-contents/> to clear all shapes and fluids from the selected machines and platforms.
When selecting a <gll:WKBelts_Intro>Conveyor Belt</gll>, the entire connected belt is cleared - not just the single selected tile.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_FloorVisibility.title: Level Visibility
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_FloorVisibility.text-in: Press to toggle the Visibility of Levels above the current level. This works both in Machine View and Space View.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Pipette.title: Pipette / Eyedropper
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Pipette.text-intro-1: Move the cursor over a <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machine</gll> or <gll:WKIslands_SpacePlatforms>Platform</gll> and press <hotkey:mass-selection.pipette/> to quickly select it in the <gll:WKPlacement_Toolbar>Toolbar</gll> for placement.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_Pipette.text-robot: Pipette, Eyedropper, Quick Clone, Pick, Picker - nobody knows the real name. We just press the button.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_SelectConnected.title: Select Connected
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_SelectConnected.text-in: Select one or multiple structures and press <hotkey:mass-selection.select-connected/> to <gl>Expand</gl> a selection to all connected structures.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_SelectConnected.text-ro: If you select one belt and it selects your entire factory, it might be time to start over.
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_FilterSelection.title: Filtering Selection
Entries.wiki.WKPlacement_FilterSelection.text-in: After selecting an <gll:WKPlacement_AreaSelection>Area</gll>, click <hotkey:building-placement.confirm-placement/> an icon in the side panel to filter the selection. Use <hotkey:mass-selection.select-all-of-building-type-modifier/> + <hotkey:building-placement.confirm-placement/> to filter the selection to include only structures of that type.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Intro.title: Conveyor Belts
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Intro.text-intro-1: Conveyor Belts transport Shapes between Machines. Belts are much faster than machines, so one belt can supply multiple machines at once. Drag from or towards existing belts to automatically Split or Merge them.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Intro.text-robot: Belts don't judge. They'll happily carry the wrong shape to the wrong place at maximum speed.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Placement.title: Belt Placement
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Placement.text-intro-1: Select a belt and drag with <hotkey:building-placement.confirm-placement/> to place it.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Checkpoints.title: Anchors
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Checkpoints.text-intro-1:
Place <gl>Anchors</gl> <icon:Checkpoint/> with <hotkey:building-placement.place-checkpoint/> to easily create belt paths with multiple turns in one go.

This also works for <gll:WKIslands_SpaceBelts>Space Belts</gll>, <gll:WKFluids_SpacePipes>Space Pipes</gll>, <gll:WKFluids_Pipes>Pipes</gll>, <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Wires</gll> and <gll:WKTrains_Rails>Rails</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Checkpoints.text-robot: Anchors let you place complex paths in one go. The previous method involved 47 individual clicks and a strong sense of optimism.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Splitting.title: Splitting Belts
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Splitting.text-intro-1: Drag from existing belts to automatically Split them. Splitting belts is necessary because machine processing slows down belts.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Merging.title: Merging Belts
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Merging.text-intro-1: Drag towards existing belts to automatically Merge them. You can merge the output of multiple machines, since conveyor belts are much faster than machines.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Lifts.title: Belt Lifts
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_Lifts.text-intro-1: Once you have unlocked the second Level, you can transport items between levels and even place machines there. While dragging a belt, press to move one level up and to move one level down. This will automatically place a Conveyor Lift.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_IdealRatios.title: Ideal Ratios
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_IdealRatios.text-intro-1: Machines are much slower than Conveyor Belts, so you need multiples of each machine per belt. Select a machine and check Machines Per Full Belt in the Details Panel to see the exact ratio. For example, on the right you can see that 3 Half Destroyers match one belt. If you place too few machines per belt, the belt will stall and stop moving - meaning you aren't reaching full Throughput. If you place too many, the extra machines will sit idle and waste space.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_IdealRatios.text-robot: Some Operators eyeball it. The efficient ones check the ratio first.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Intro.title: Blueprints
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Intro.text-intro-1:
<gl>Blueprints</gl> let you copy and paste sections of your factory.

<gll:WKPlacement_AreaSelection>Select an area</gll> with <hotkey:mass-selection.select-area-modifier/> <hotkey:mass-selection.select-base/>, then press <hotkey:mass-selection.make-blueprint/> to copy it.

This works in both <gll:WKProcessing_BuildingMode>Machine View</gll> and <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll>, letting you copy individual <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll> or entire <gll:WKIslands_SpacePlatforms>Space Platforms</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Intro.text-robot: Build once, paste forever. The lazy Operator's best friend. Not that I'm calling you lazy.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Library.title: Blueprint Library
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Library.text-intro-1:
Save any blueprint to the <gl>Blueprint Library</gl> with <hotkey:building-placement.save-blueprint/>.

Access the library with <hotkey:main.toggle-blueprint-library/> or the button at the top-right. Right-click saved blueprints to edit them.

Blueprints are stored on disk (use <gl>Open Folder</gl> to find them) and shared across all savegames.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Library.text-robot: Your blueprints persist across all savegames. So does the one you saved at 3 AM and named 'asdfjkl.'
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Sharing.title: Sharing Blueprints
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Sharing.text-intro-1: Blueprints are copied as <gl>Blueprint Code</gl> to your system clipboard by default, so you can share them with others.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Sharing.text-robot: Sharing is caring. Vortex LTD assumes no liability for designs obtained from other Operators.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Icons.title: Blueprint Icons
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Icons.text-intro-1:
You can make it easier to identify blueprints by setting a <gl>Custom Icon</gl> during blueprint creation or when editing.

The icons are also shown and useful when binding a blueprint to the <gll:WKBlueprints_Toolbar>Blueprint Toolbar</gll>.

You can select up to 4 icons per blueprint. If you only assign one icon, it will be shown enlarged.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Icons.text-custom-shap: You can also use custom Shapes as icons by entering a Shape Code. Otherwise invalid shape codes are allowed here - for example, shapes with just 3 parts like CuCuCu work just fine.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Flipping.title: Mirroring Blueprints
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Flipping.text-intro-1:
Blueprints can be <gll:WKPlacement_Mirroring>Mirrored</gll> with <hotkey:building-placement.mirror/>.

Use <hotkey:building-placement.mirror-inverse/> to mirror on the other axis.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Folders.title: Blueprint Folders
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Folders.text-intro-1: Create folders in the Blueprint Library to organize your blueprints. Folders can be nested and are also created on your disk. You can also bind entire folders to the Blueprint Toolbar for quick access.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Toolbar.title: Blueprint Toolbar
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Toolbar.text-intro-1:
Any blueprint and <gll:WKBlueprints_Folders>Blueprint Folder</gll> can be placed in the <gl>Blueprint Toolbar</gl> for quick access.

Select a blueprint in the <gll:WKBlueprints_Library>Blueprint Library</gll> and then drag it into the toolbar to assign it.

You can also set <gll:WKBlueprints_Icons>Custom Icons</gll> on your blueprints to keep things organized.
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_PasteLast.title: Paste Last Blueprint
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_PasteLast.text-intro-1: Press to re-select your most recent blueprint. This requires the Copy Blueprints to Clipboard option to be enabled (on by default).
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Cost.title: Blueprint Cost
Entries.wiki.WKBlueprints_Cost.text-intro-1:
<gll:WKBlueprints_Intro>Blueprints</gll> are free to place.

In <gll:WKMisc_Difficulty>Challenge</gll> difficulty only, placing blueprints costs <gl>Blueprint Credits</gl>, which you earn by delivering specific shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Shapes.title: Shapes
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Shapes.text-intro-1:
<gl>Shapes</gl> are made up of <gl>Quarters</gl> that can be separated and reorganized.

Like cakes, shapes can have multiple <gl>Layers</gl>, each with up to 4 distinct quarters. New quarter types can be found farther away from the center.

Different <gll:WKMisc_GameModes>Game Modes</gll> have different shape types available.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Shapes.text-robot: Circles, Squares, Stars, Diamonds. R&D briefly considered a fifth shape. We don't talk about the fifth shape.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_ShapeViewer.title: Shape Inspector
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_ShapeViewer.text-intro-1: You can inspect shapes in the <gl>Shape Inspector</gl> by clicking <hotkey:building-placement.confirm-placement/> almost any shape in the user interface.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Pins.title: Support Pins
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Pins.text-intro-1: Unconnected quarters fall when <gll:WKProcessing_Stacker>Stacked</gll>. You can use the <gll:WKProcessing_PinPusher>Pin Pusher</gll> to add <gl>Support Pins</gl>, preventing this and letting you build shapes that would otherwise be impossible.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Pins.text-robot: Project STILTS was supposed to be a simple structural fix. Three research phases later, the lab staff were reassigned and their desks were already empty. The pins work great, though.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Crystals.title: Crystals
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Crystals.text-intro-1:
You can use the <gll:WKFluids_CrystalGenerator>Crystal Generator</gll> to crystallize gaps in shapes, creating <gl>Crystals</gl>. Crystals are <gl>Fragile</gl>, and thus break when falling down, for example while <gll:WKProcessing_Stacker>Stacked</gll>.

<gl>Cutting</gl> through a crystal will <gl>Shatter</gl> it, destroying all connected crystals on the same, upper and lower shape layers.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Crystals.text-robot: FRAGILE - DO NOT DROP, BREAK OR EAT.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Crystals.text-intro-2: While stacking crystals breaks them, you can use the Swapper to swap halves, as long as you don't cut through them. You can Break Crystals during swapping to create shapes with gaps below crystals - but this is only required in the Hard and Insane scenarios.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Code.title: Shape Codes
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Code.text-intro-1: Every shape has a unique Shape Code - used in the Shape Inspector, Signal Producer, and Location Markers. Each quarter is encoded as an uppercase letter for the shape type and one for the color. Layers are separated by ':', and empty parts are '--'. Available shapes and colors depend on the Game Mode.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Code.text-examples: CuCuCuCu - Full Circle CuCu---- - Half Circle RrRrRrRr - Full red Square RuRu----:CuCu---- - Half Circle on top of half Square
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Code.text-robot: Every shape has a code. Every code has a shape. Except one. Anomaly #221 had a code that was longer than our systems could parse. But you didn't hear that from me.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Predictions.title: Shape Previews
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Predictions.text-intro-1: Shape Previews show produced Shapes before they arrive, so you can build ahead without waiting. Since they show what will eventually be produced, they may differ from what's currently on a belt. Previews work everywhere, predicting even Trains.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Predictions.text-intro-2: Shape Previews indicate whether you produce the correct shapes: - Gray: This shape doesn't serve any specific purpose. - Checkmark: Correct shape, keep delivering! - Flag: You delivered enough. - Orange Lock: Correct, but future goal. - Purple Laurel: Operator Level Shape, keep delivering! - Blueprint: Blueprint shape (Challenge difficulty only).
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Predictions.text-robot: The preview system knows what your factory will produce before it produces it. It does not know why you built it that way. But you know what you're doing, right?
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_ShapeLayerLimit.title: Shape Layer Limit
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_ShapeLayerLimit.text-intro:
<gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shapes</gll> can have at most 4 <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Layers</gll> (5 in the <gl>Insane</gl> scenario). Stacking beyond the limit destroys excess layers.

This can actually be used to your advantage in later stages - keep it in mind.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_ShapeLayerLimit.text-robot: Four layers ought to be enough for anybody. Famous last words.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingDiagonals.title: Creating Diagonals
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingDiagonals.text-int: There are multiple ways to create <gl>Diagonal Shapes</gl> (shapes with quarters on opposite corners, like <icon:Cu--Cu--/>, also called <gl>Checkered</gl> shapes).
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingDiagonals.text-sim: The simplest method is to create two <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Quarters</gll> first (e.g. <icon:Cu------/> and <icon:----Cu--/>) and then <gll:WKProcessing_Stacker>Stack</gll> them together to produce <icon:Cu--Cu--/>.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingDiagonals.text-cut: The <gll:WKProcessing_Cutter>Cutter</gll> outputs both halves, allowing you to rotate one half and stack them together more efficiently.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingDiagonals.text-swa: The <gll:WKProcessing_Swapper>Swapper</gll> exchanges the west halves of two shapes, enabling even more efficient diagonal creation by avoiding the need for separate cutting operations.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Overhangs.title: Overhangs
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Overhangs.text-intro-1: When shapes are Stacked, quarters that are not supported will fall down. However, quarters that are connected to supported quarters will stay in place, creating Overhangs.
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_Overhangs.text-connection-: A quarter counts as connected when it is adjacent to another quarter either on the same layer (e.g. is connected, but is not) or directly above/below on another layer (e.g. the top half circle of is connected to its bottom half square).
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Intro.title: Machines
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Intro.text-intro-1: <gl>Machines</gl> process <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shapes</gll> and <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluids</gll>. Place them in <gll:WKProcessing_BuildingMode>Machine View</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_BuildingMode.title: Machine View
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_BuildingMode.text-intr:
In the <gl>Machine View</gl> you can build <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll>.

Press <hotkey:main.scope-change/> or zoom <hotkey:camera.angle-drag-modifier/> to toggle between <gl>Machine View</gl> and <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Floors.title: Machine Levels
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Floors.text-intro-1: Machine Levels let you build Machines and Conveyor Belts on multiple vertical levels, giving you more space to work with. Use and to Switch between Levels. Use Belt Lifts to transport shapes between them.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Floors.text-intro-2: You start with two levels and can unlock a 3rd level later on.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Floors.text-robot: More levels, more room for organized factories. Right?
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Extractor.title: Mini-Miner
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Extractor.text-intro-1: The Mini-Miner extracts Shapes from Asteroid Miner Platforms. Connect Miner Boosters to the Asteroid Miner Platform to unlock additional extraction spots, up to 16 per miner. Each Mini-Miner extracts a fixed amount of shapes per minute - select one to view its rate.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_HalfDestroyer.title: Half Destroyer
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_HalfDestroyer.text-int: The Half Destroyer uses the isolator laser to disintegrate the West Half of a shape, outputting anything that remains. Unlike the Cutter, the destroyed half is not output - it's gone for good. This makes it useful when you only need one half of a shape and don't want to deal with leftover waste. Chain it with a Rotator and another Half Destroyer to create Quarters - half cut, rotate, then half cut again.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_HalfDestroyer.text-rob: Destroying half of every shape feels wasteful. There has to be a better way. Right? ...Right?
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingQuarters.title: Creating Quarters
Entries.wiki.WKShapes_CreatingQuarters.text-intr: By rotating a <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shape</gll> and combining machines, you can control which half is cut. This allows you to cut, rotate and cut again to produce <gl>Quarters</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Rotator.title: Rotator
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Rotator.text-intro-sha: Rotators change the Orientation of shapes, which is essential for processing them correctly. Most machines operate on a fixed side - the Cutter, Half Destroyer and Swapper all work on the West Half. By rotating a shape before processing, you choose which side gets modified.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Rotator.text-intro-1: The default Rotator rotates by 90 degrees clockwise, giving you access to all four orientations.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Rotator.text-intro-1-h: The default Rotator rotates by 60 degrees clockwise, giving you access to all six orientations.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Rotator.text-more-vari: Unlock additional variants for counter-clockwise and 180-degree rotation in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Cutter.title: Cutter
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Cutter.text-intro-1: The Cutter can be unlocked in the Shop and uses a holographic blade to split a Shape in two, separating the West Half from the East Half and outputting both. Both halves can be processed further - feed them into a Stacker to recombine them, or use a Rotator before cutting to control which quarters end up on which side. If you only need one half, consider the Half Destroyer instead - it destroys the west half and saves you from dealing with unwanted output.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Cutter.text-robot: The holographic blade is perfectly safe. Probably. Just don't look directly at it.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_PinPusher.title: Pin Pusher
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_PinPusher.text-intro-1: The Pin Pusher pushes a Support Pin under every non-empty Quarter on the first layer. Pins support quarters above them and can be stacked themselves. This lets you create otherwise impossible shapes - with large gaps and sections elevated by multiple layers.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_PinPusher.text-robot: Floating shapes are dead, long live floating shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Swapper.title: Swapper
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Swapper.text-intro-1: The Swapper can be unlocked in the Shop and uses two holographic blades to simultaneously split shapes and swap their West Halves, then outputs both. As long as the cut doesn't go through a Crystal, the Swapper preserves them intact. This makes it useful - and sometimes required - for assembling more complex shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Swapper.text-robot: Looks confusing at first. Then it stays confusing.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Stacker.title: Stacker
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Stacker.text-intro-1: The Stacker combines shapes through Stacking - producing shapes with multiple Layers or combining Quarters into a single layer. It receives shapes from two inputs - the upper shape is dropped on top of the lower, and any disconnected sections fall until they hit a solid surface. If both shapes are whole, they stack neatly. With empty or loose quarters, parts may fall further before landing. The Stacker requires Belt Lifts to feed its second input on the upper Level.
Entries.wiki.WKProcessing_Stacker.text-robot: The Stacker is slow. Very slow. You'll need a lot of them. I mean a LOT.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Intro.title: Fluids
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Intro.text-intro-1:
<gl>Color Fluids</gl> are required to paint <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shapes</gll>, introducing multiple new concepts.

Extract fluids using <gll:WKFluids_FluidMiners>Asteroid Pumps</gll>, place a <gll:WKFluids_Pump>Mini Pump</gll> on them, transport fluids via <gll:WKFluids_SpacePipes>Space Pipes</gll> and <gll:WKFluids_Pipes>Pipes</gll>, then use a <gll:WKFluids_Painting>Painter</gll> to color your shapes.

Fluid volume is measured in <gl>Liters</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Intro.text-robot:
You can switch this to gallons in the settings.

... just kidding.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Pump.title: Mini Pump
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Pump.text-intro-1:
Like <gll:WKProcessing_Extractor>Mini-Miners</gll>, <gl>Mini Pumps</gl> extract <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluids</gll> from <gll:WKFluids_FluidMiners>Asteroid Pump Platforms</gll>.

Add <gll:WKFluids_FluidMiners>Pump Boosters</gll> to unlock additional extraction spots, up to 16.

Each <gl>Mini Pump</gl> extracts a fixed amount of liters per minute - select one to view its rate.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Pipes.title: Pipes
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Pipes.text-intro-1: Pipes are required to transport fluids. Transportation happens instantly with infinite throughput - but only within the same platform. To move fluids between platforms, you need Fluid Launchers. These have a limited throughput, so they're the bottleneck you should design your factory around.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidLaunchers.title: Fluid Launchers
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidLaunchers.text-intro-: The Fluid Launcher works the same way as Belt Launchers - just for fluids. Place a matching Fluid Catcher to receive the fluids on the other end. They can launch over machines or between platforms, with a maximum range of 5 tiles. Unlike Pipes, Fluid Launchers have a limited throughput. Machines that use fluids (like the Painter) show a Machines per Fluid Launcher stat - use this to plan how many launchers you need.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidLaunchers.text-robot: Somewhere, a physicist is crying about what we do to fluid dynamics. Good thing this is a simulation.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidMiners.title: Asteroid Pumps & Boosters
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidMiners.text-intro-1:
<gl>Asteroid Pump Platforms</gl> extract <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluids</gll> from <gl>Fluid Asteroids</gl>, similar to <gll:WKIslands_ShapeMiners>Asteroid Miners</gll>.

Add up to 3 <gl>Pump Boosters</gl> to increase throughput. Each booster unlocks 4 more <gl>Extraction Spots</gl>, up to 16.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_SpacePipes.title: Space Pipes
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_SpacePipes.text-intro-1:
<gl>Space Pipes</gl> transport fluids between platforms - regular <gll:WKIslands_SpaceBelts>Space Belts</gll> can't carry fluids.

To get fluids into a space pipe, <gll:WKFluids_FluidLaunchers>Fluid Launchers</gll> package them into small fluid blobs. These are placed automatically when you drag a space pipe towards a platform's input or output.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_SpacePipes.text-intro-2: Just like Space Belts, each space pipe has 4 lanes per level, for a total of 12 lanes across all three Machine Levels. Lanes never mix - a fluid entering on a specific level and side will always exit on that same level and side, no matter how you split, merge, or reroute the space pipe. To use all 12 lanes you need to have unlocked the 3rd Machine Level.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Painting.title: Painter
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Painting.text-intro-1: Connect both fluid and shapes to a Painter to paint the shape. The painter always paints the topmost layer of a given shape. Painting a shape always consumes 10 liters of fluid, no matter if the shape already had the same color or has fewer than 4 quarters. This means it's more efficient to paint before cutting - paint a full shape once, then Cut it into halves or quarters, instead of painting each piece individually.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Mixing.title: Color Mixing
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_Mixing.text-intro-1:
By combining two colors with the <gl>Color Mixer</gl> you can create <gl>Secondary</gl> and <gl>Tertiary</gl> colors.

The <gl>Fluid Volume</gl> is preserved, so if you mix 10 liters of red and 10 liters of green, you will get 20 liters of yellow.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidTank.title: Fluid Tank
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidTank.text-intro-1: The Fluid Tank stores up to 3,600 liters of any Fluid and is directional. It has a limited input and output throughput. In most cases, a steady flow beats buffering. Tanks are best used as a last resort.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_FluidTank.text-robot: A tank doesn't fix throughput problems. It just stores them for later. Ask me how I know.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_CrystalGenerator.title: Crystal Generator
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_CrystalGenerator.text-intr:
The <gl>Crystal Generator</gl> fills <gl>Gaps</gl> and <gll:WKShapes_Pins>Support Pins</gll> in a shape with fluid, hardening it into <gll:WKShapes_Crystals>Crystal</gll>.

Chain multiple generators to combine different colored crystals in one shape.

Keep in mind that <gll:WKShapes_Crystals>Crystals</gll> are fragile - they break when falling down, for example while <gll:WKProcessing_Stacker>Stacking</gll>. <gll:WKProcessing_Cutter>Cutting</gll> through a crystal will shatter it, destroying all connected crystals. Use the <gll:WKProcessing_Swapper>Swapper</gll> to rearrange halves without breaking them, as long as the cut doesn't go through a crystal.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_CrystalGenerator.text-robo: Pour fluid into gaps, get crystals. R&D hasn't explained how. We've stopped asking.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_ColorMode.title: Color Mode
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_ColorMode.text-intro-1: By default, colors use <gl>Additive Color Mixing</gl>, which works just like light: mixing red, green and blue together makes white.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_ColorMode.text-intro-2: If you prefer alternative schemes like RYB or CMYK, you can change the <gl>Color Mode</gl> in the settings any time.
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_ColorBlindness.title: Color Blindness
Entries.wiki.WKFluids_ColorBlindness.text-intro-: If you have trouble distinguishing the colors below (every pipe contains a different color), you might be <gl>Colorblind</gl>.
You can switch the color scheme to RGB (Color blind) in the settings to be able to distinguish the colors.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicVortex_Vortex.title: The Vortex
Entries.wiki.WKClassicVortex_Vortex.text-intro-1:
Deliver <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shapes</gll> to the <gl>Vortex</gl> to complete <gll:WKResearch_Milestones>Milestones</gll> and <gll:WKResearchClassic_SideTasks>Jobs</gll> to earn various rewards.

You can deliver shapes from <gll:WKProcessing_Floors>Multiple Levels</gll> to the <gl>Vortex</gl>. If that still is not sufficient, you can unlock more <gl>Vortex Inputs</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicVortex_Vortex.text-robot: It consumes everything you give it and always wants more. Reminds me of someone, but I can't quite place who.
Entries.wiki.WKClassicVortex_ShapeRotation.title: Shape Orientation
Entries.wiki.WKClassicVortex_ShapeRotation.text-: The Vortex accepts any shape orientation, so there is no need to rotate the shapes beforehand.
Why rotate the shape when you can just rotate the camera. Engineering at its finest.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Milestones.title: Milestones
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Milestones.text-classic:
<gl>Milestones</gl> unlock new major technologies like <gll:WKTrains_Intro>Trains</gll> or <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluids</gll>.

Complete milestones by delivering the required shapes to the <gll:WKClassicVortex_Vortex>Vortex</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Milestones.text-converte:
<gl>Milestones</gl> unlock new <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll>, <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll>, and other technologies.

Complete milestones by delivering the required shapes to the appropriate <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKResearchClassic_SideTasks.title: Jobs
Entries.wiki.WKResearchClassic_SideTasks.text-in: Complete Jobs to earn Platform Capacity and Research Credits. Deliver the requested shapes to the Vortex. That's it. Jobs evolve progressively - each step typically builds on the previous one. The next goal often uses the shape from the current goal with some modification, so you can expand your existing setup instead of building a new factory from scratch.
Entries.wiki.WKResearchClassic_SideTasks.text-ro: Almost feels like a real job. And you get paid for it... right?
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Shop.title: Shop
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Shop.text-classic: Spend your <gll:WKResearch_ResearchPoints><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gll> in the <gl>Shop</gl> to purchase or upgrade technologies.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_Shop.text-converter: Spend your <gll:WKResearch_ResearchPoints><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gll> in the <gl>Shop</gl> to purchase or upgrade technologies.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_ResearchPoints.title: Research Credits
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_ResearchPoints.text-clas: <gl><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gl> are rewarded for completing <gll:WKResearchClassic_SideTasks>Jobs</gll> or increasing your <gll:WKResearch_OperatorLevel>Operator Level</gll> and can be spent in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_ResearchPoints.text-conv: <gl><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gl> are earned by increasing your <gll:WKResearch_OperatorLevel>Operator Level</gll> and can be spent in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKResearchClassic_PinTasks.title: Pinning Jobs
Entries.wiki.WKResearchClassic_PinTasks.text-int:
<gl>Pin</gl> a <gll:WKResearchClassic_SideTasks>Job</gll> via the pin icon in the <gll:WKResearchClassic_SideTasks>Jobs Screen</gll> to track its shape on the left side of the screen.

Delivering any shape of a job automatically pins it.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_OperatorLevel.title: Operator Level
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_OperatorLevel.text-class:
Deliver milestone shapes to increase your <gl>Operator Level</gl>. Each level grants <gll:WKResearch_ResearchPoints><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gll>, <gll:WKIslands_PlatformLimit><icon:ChunkLimit/> Platform Capacity</gll>, and bragging rights.

Delivering the same shape has diminishing returns - try to deliver all of them equally.

After the final milestone, you unlock <gl>Random Operator Goals</gl> that change every level, encouraging you to build a <gll:WKWires_MaM>Make Anything Machine</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_OperatorLevel.text-conve:
Deliver shapes to <gll:WKTradeStations_ResearchStation>Research Stations</gll> and <gll:WKTradeStations_DonationStation>Donation Stations</gll> to increase your <gl>Operator Level</gl>. Each level grants <gll:WKResearch_ResearchPoints><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gll> and <gll:WKIslands_PlatformLimit><icon:ChunkLimit/> Platform Capacity</gll>.

The <gll:WKTradeStations_ResearchStation>Research Station</gll> also returns byproducts needed for higher-tier <gll:WKTradeStations_Overview>Trade Stations</gll>.

Delivering the same shape has diminishing returns - try to deliver all of them equally.
Entries.wiki.WKResearch_OperatorLevel.text-robot: Every level you gain means you're more valuable to the Vortex. Not to the company. To the Vortex. There's a difference. Probably.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceMode.title: Space View
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceMode.text-intro-1: Zoom out with <hotkey:camera.angle-drag-modifier/> or press <hotkey:main.scope-change/> to enter the <gl>Space View</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpacePlatforms.title: Space Platforms
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpacePlatforms.text-intro:
Unlock <gl>Space-Building</gl> to expand beyond the initial platforms.

Enter <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll> by zooming out or pressing <hotkey:main.scope-change/>.

<gll:WKIslands_Foundations>Machine Platforms</gll> provide space for <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll>, while specialized structures like <gll:WKIslands_ShapeMiners>Asteroid Miners</gll> serve distinct purposes.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_PlatformLimit.title: Platform Capacity
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_PlatformLimit.text-classi: Building in space is limited by your Platform Capacity. Increase it by completing Jobs or leveling up your Operator Level. In Relaxed difficulty, building in space is unlimited. You can also purchase the following upgrade in the Shop for additional capacity.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_PlatformLimit.text-conver: Building in space is limited by your Platform Capacity, which can be increased primarily through Operator Level rewards and upgrades in the Shop. In Relaxed difficulty, building in space is unlimited.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_Floors.title: Space Levels
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_Floors.text-intro-1: Similar to Machine Levels, you can build on multiple levels in space. Switch levels with and , and use Level Visibility to hide levels above the current one. In the beginning there are two space levels, but as you progress you will be able to unlock a 3rd Space Level.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_Floors.text-settings: The Auto Change Space Level setting (enabled by default) automatically switches to the correct space level as you zoom into Machine View. The Change Space Level In Machine View setting lets you cycle through space levels while in Machine View - when you go past the top or bottom machine level with / , it jumps to the next space level.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_ShapeMiners.title: Asteroid Miners & Boosters
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_ShapeMiners.text-intro-1:
<gl>Asteroid Miner Platforms</gl> can only be placed on <gl>Shape Asteroids</gl>. Place <gll:WKProcessing_Extractor>Mini-Miners</gll> on them to extract shapes.

Add up to 3 <gl>Miner Boosters</gl> to increase throughput. Each booster unlocks 4 more <gl>Extraction Spots</gl>, up to 16.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceBelts.title: Space Belts
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceBelts.text-intro-1: Space Belts work like regular belts but with much higher throughput, letting you merge output from multiple platforms. Each space belt has 4 lanes per level, for a total of 12 lanes across all three Machine Levels. Lanes never mix - an item entering on a specific level and side will always exit on that same level and side, no matter how you split, merge, or reroute the space belt. To use all 12 lanes you need to have unlocked the 3rd Machine Level.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceBelts.text-robot: 12 lanes, zero mixing. If you need more than that, it might be time to rethink your layout.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceBeltLifts.title: Space Belt Lifts
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_SpaceBeltLifts.text-intro:
<gl>Space Belt Lifts</gl> can be placed just as regular belt lifts - use them to connect space belts across separate levels.

There are no 'space belt launchers' - dragging space belts over obstacles will instead automatically place <gl>Space Belt Lifts</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_Foundations.title: Machine Platforms
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_Foundations.text-intro-1:
Place <gl>Machine Platforms</gl> in the <gll:WKIslands_SpaceMode>Space View</gll> to create more space for your <gll:WKProcessing_Intro>Machines</gll>.

It is recommended to think of these as <gll:WKIslands_ModularBuilds>Modules</gll> so you can reuse them.

There are multiple platform variants available in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_ModularBuilds.title: Thinking Modular
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_ModularBuilds.text-intro-: Group parts of your factory into reusable Modules and save them as Blueprints. Think of platforms as large machines - apply the same concepts on a bigger scale. For example: build a platform that takes 4 belts of shapes, rotates all of them, and outputs them on the other side.
Voila - you now have your own reusable <gl>Space Rotator Module</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKIslands_ModularBuilds.text-robot: Spaghetti factories are a cry for help. Modular factories are a cry for efficiency. Choose wisely.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Intro.title: Train Basics
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Intro.text-intro-1: Trains provide high-throughput, long-distance transport. Space Belts work fine for short distances, but trains are much faster over long stretches - they take a bit more effort to set up, but once running they move at high speed. On top of that, Rails are free and don't consume Platform Capacity.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Intro.text-intro-2: An example of a minimal train system:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Intro.text-intro-3: 1. A Locomotive Depot that spawns the train and sets its color. 2. One or more Wagon Depots placed behind the locomotive. 3. Rails connecting source to destination. 4. A Train Stop with Wagon Loaders at the source. 5. A Train Stop with Wagon Unloaders at the destination.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Intro.text-robot: Warning: trains are highly addictive. Side effects include building elaborate rail networks when a single belt would have sufficed.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Rails.title: Rails
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Rails.text-intro-1: Trains run on Rails. Place them like Space Belts or Space Pipes - they can also switch between Space Levels. Multiple trains - even of different colors - can share the same rail. Rails can be split and merged to create intersections. At a split, each train follows the direction configured for its color via Train Routing.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Rails.text-intro-2: A merge on the top side acts as a split on the bottom side. Trains don't track where they came from - they always follow the Route matching their color.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Rails.text-speed: Trains slowly accelerate but slow down on turns. Build long straight tracks for maximum speed. Train speed can be upgraded in the Shop:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Locomotives.title: Locomotives
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Locomotives.text-intro-1: Every train starts with a Locomotive Depot. Its color determines the entire train's color, which controls where a train goes when it approaches a Rail Intersection. Place Wagon Depots behind the locomotive to configure the train. A train will only spawn once at least one wagon is connected and the depot is connected to a Rail.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Locomotives.text-intro-2: Additional train colors can be unlocked in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Locomotives.text-robot: Seven colors, seven routes. Engineering wanted eight, but accounting said no.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Wagons.title: Wagons
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Wagons.text-intro-1: Place as many Wagon Depots as you'd like behind a Locomotive Depot. For every depot placed, the train will have a matching wagon.
