There are up to three types of wagons, unlockable in the Shop: - Shape Wagons carry shapes compressed into packages - Fluid Wagons carry fluids compressed into packages - Spacer Wagons create trains with gaps for more flexible stations A train can have mixed wagon types - for example, shape and fluid wagons on the same train.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_Wagons.text-capacity: By default, each wagon holds one package per Machine Level. You can also upgrade the capacity of the train wagons in the Shop:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.title: Shape Transport
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.text-intro-: Place a Shape Wagon Depot behind a Locomotive Depot to add a shape wagon to your train. Shapes are compressed into packages for transport. Use a loader at the source and an unloader at the destination.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.text-wagons: Each depot adds one wagon. Place as many as you need.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.text-loader: Compresses shapes from Space Belts into packages of 360 shapes each and loads them onto the train. Place before a Train Stop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.text-unload: Unpacks shape packages back onto <gll:WKIslands_SpaceBelts>Space Belts</gll>. Place before a <gll:WKTrains_TrainStops>Train Stop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_ShapeTransport.text-transf: Transfers packages directly between two shape wagons - no unpacking needed.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.title: Fluid Transport
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.text-intro-: Place a Fluid Wagon Depot behind a Locomotive Depot to add a fluid wagon to your train. Fluids are compressed into packages for transport. Use a loader at the source and an unloader at the destination.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.text-wagons: Each depot adds one wagon. Place as many as you need.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.text-loader: Compresses fluids from Space Pipes into packages of 3,600 liters each and loads them onto the train. Place before a Train Stop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.text-unload: Unpacks fluid packages back onto <gll:WKFluids_SpacePipes>Space Pipes</gll>. Place before a <gll:WKTrains_TrainStops>Train Stop</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_FluidTransport.text-transf: Transfers packages directly between two fluid wagons - no unpacking needed.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SpacerWagons.title: Spacer Wagons
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SpacerWagons.text-intro-1: Spacer Wagons can be unlocked in the Shop. They don't carry any cargo, but create gaps in the train so you can control which wagons get loaded at a station.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SpacerWagons.text-example: For example, a Train Station with 3 Shape Loaders can serve multiple trains. One train might have 3 shape wagons and load all 3. Another train could use a Spacer Wagon in the first slot and only load the second and third wagon at that same station.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStations.title: Train Stations
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStations.text-intro-1: To build a Train Station, place Wagon Loaders or Wagon Unloaders on a rail, then end with a Train Stop. Stations must be continuous without gaps between platforms. A station can be shorter or longer than the train.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStations.text-station: To selectively load or unload specific wagons, use Station Spacers to create gaps in your station. For example, to only unload the second wagon, place a Wagon Unloader, then a Station Spacer, then the Train Stop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStops.title: Train Stops
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStops.text-intro-1: A Train Stop is the foremost element of a Train Station. Place it on a rail to signal the locomotive to halt. Wagon Loaders and Wagon Unloaders go before the stop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStops.text-intro-2: There are two types of stops available:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStops.text-quick-stop: Trains will only stop at the <gl>Immediate Stop</gl> if they can be immediately loaded or unloaded. Otherwise they will ignore the stop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainStops.text-wait-stop: The Wait Stop can be unlocked in the Shop. Trains will always stop there, until they are either fully loaded or unloaded, or 60 seconds have passed.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainRoutes.title: Train Routing
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainRoutes.text-intro-1: When trains reach an intersection, they choose the direction based on the route configured for their color. The Rails show an indicator of where trains of which color will go.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainRoutes.text-intro-2: Select a Route Configurator with the intended color from the toolbar and drag over an existing Rail Intersection to change the direction trains of that color will go. This way you can make red and blue trains choose different paths:
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainRoutes.text-intro-3: If no specific route is configured, trains will prioritize going forward, right or left, in that order.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_TrainRoutes.text-robot: Red goes left. Blue goes right. Unassigned trains go forward. They always go forward. Even when there's no track. ...Especially when there's no track.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_VortexDelivery.title: Train Vortex Delivery
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_VortexDelivery.text-intro-:
After unlocking the <gl>Vortex Delivery</gl> milestone, you can launch trains directly into the <gll:WKClassicVortex_Vortex>Vortex</gll>, delivering all their contents at once.

The train will respawn shortly after.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_VortexDelivery.text-robot: The driver is safe. Don't worry about it.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SupporterEdition.title: Rollercoaster Pack
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SupporterEdition.text-intr: The shapez 2 Supporter Edition (DLC) unlocks purely cosmetic rails and new music - it's a way to support us as developers. If you own it, the decorations can be unlocked in the Shop.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SupporterEdition.text-loop: The <gl>Rail Loop</gl> makes trains loop. You can also launch trains through it.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SupporterEdition.text-twis: The <gl>Twister</gl> makes trains do a twist.
Entries.wiki.WKTrains_SupporterEdition.text-robo: Can you actually build a twister THROUGH a loop? ...Only one way to find out.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WiresSignals.title: Wires & Signals
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WiresSignals.text-intro-1:
<gl>Wires</gl> let you fully automate factories - even building dynamic factories that produce any shape (<gll:WKWires_MaM>Make Anything Machine</gll>, or <gll:WKWires_MaM>MaM</gll>).

Wire buildings transport and process <gl>Logical Signals</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WiresSignals.text-intro-2: A logical signal can either be a: - Number: 0, 1, 2, 3, ... - Color: Red (r), Green (g), Blue (b), ... - Shape: Any shape, i.e. - Null: Also called 'no signal', the default state of any wire. - Conflict: Whenever signals conflict, i.e. connecting two outputs with different signals.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Truthiness.title: Truthiness
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Truthiness.text-intro-1: <gl>Empty</gl>, <gl>Conflict</gl> and the number <gl>0</gl> are classified as <gl>Off</gl>. All other signal values are considered as <gl>On</gl>. This is important for logic gates.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_ConstantSignal.title: Signal Producer
Entries.wiki.WKWires_ConstantSignal.text-intro-1: The Signal Producer can be configured to produce any logical signal. Select it and click Configure to set the output.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_ConstantSignal.text-intro-2: - Number: Enter a number (1, 2, 3, ...) to emit a Number Signal - Color: Enter a color code (e.g. color-r) to emit a color - Shape: Enter a Shape Code (e.g. CuCuCuCu) to emit a shape - null: Emits no signal - conflict: Emits a Conflict signal
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.title: Belt Filter
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.text-intro-1:
The <gl>Belt Filter</gl> allows you to filter shapes based on various criteria, depending on the <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Signal</gll> input.

If the input signal is <gl>Null</gl> or <gl>Conflict</gl>, the filter does not forward any shapes.

If the input signal is a <gl>Number Signal</gl>, the filter acts as a gate and will only forward shapes if the signal is not 0.

If the input signal is a <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signal</gll>, the filter forwards all shapes that match the signal, and outputs all other shapes to the side.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.text-gate: The most simple application of a belt filter is connecting a <gll:WKWires_Button>Toggle Button</gll> to its input. By toggling the button, you can control whether shapes will pass the filter or not, allowing you to control the item flow in your factory.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.text-filter: Alternatively, you can connect a <gll:WKWires_ConstantSignal>Signal Producer</gll> to the belt filter, and connect a <gl>Trash</gl> to the side output. With this setup, you can ensure that only a certain shape makes it past the filter, allowing you to filter out unwanted shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.text-robot: There used to be a sushi belt tutorial here. The Operator who wrote it has been reassigned.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltFilter.text-mam: Belt Filters are a key component for building a <gll:WKWires_MaM>Make Anything Machine (MaM)</gll> - a fully dynamic factory that can produce any shape on demand.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltReader.title: Belt Reader
Entries.wiki.WKWires_BeltReader.text-intro-1: The Belt Reader allows you to read the contents of a belt and displays its speed. The reader will always output the shape signal of the current shape, or Null otherwise.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_PipeGate.title: Pipe Gate
Entries.wiki.WKWires_PipeGate.text-intro-1:
The <gl>Pipe Gate</gl> works very similar to a <gll:WKWires_BeltFilter>Belt Filter</gll>, except for <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluids</gll>.

If the input signal is <gl>Null</gl> or <gl>Conflict</gl>, the gate does not forward any fluid.

If the input signal is a <gl>Number Signal</gl>, the gate will only forward fluid if the signal is not 0.

If the input signal is a <gl>Fluid Signal</gl>, the gate only forwards the fluid if it matches the signal.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_PipeGate.text-mam: Pipe Gates are a key component for building a <gll:WKWires_MaM>Make Anything Machine (MaM)</gll> - by controlling which fluids reach the <gl>Painter</gl>, you can dynamically paint shapes in any color on demand.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Button.title: Toggle Button
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Button.text-intro-1:
The <gl>Toggle Button</gl> is a useful tool to configure your factory, and outputs either <gl>Null</gl> or the number <gl>1</gl> when toggled.

Use <hotkey:mass-selection.toggle-button-below-cursor/> while hovering over it with your cursor to quickly toggle it.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.title: Logic Gates
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-intro-1: <gl>Logic Gates</gl> can be used to perform <gl>Boolean Operations</gl> based on the <gll:WKWires_Truthiness>Truthiness</gll> of <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Signals</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-and: The AND Gate outputs the number 1 if both input signals are Truthy, and 0 otherwise.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-or: The OR Gate outputs the number 1 if at least one or both input signals are Truthy, and 0 otherwise.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-not: The NOT Gate outputs the number 0 if the input signal is Truthy, and 1 otherwise.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-xor: The XOR Gate outputs the number 1 if one, but not both input signals are Truthy, and 0 otherwise.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-comparison: The Comparison Gate outputs the number 1 if both input signals match the specified Comparison, which can be configured as one of the following operations: - Equal - Greater Equal - Greater - Less - Less Equal - Not Equal The comparison modes Equal and Not Equal also work for Shapes and Fluids.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-if: The <gl>Transistor Gate</gl> has a special <gl>Trigger Signal</gl> input on the top and forwards the input signal only if the <gl>Trigger Signal</gl> is <gl>Truthy</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_LogicGates.text-robot: Complete your milestones AND maintain throughput OR face review. NOT a threat - just corporate policy.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_OverflowSplitter.title: Overflow Splitter
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_OverflowSplitter.text-intro:
The <gl>Overflow Splitter</gl> can be unlocked in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll> and splits a <gll:WKBelts_Intro>Conveyor Belt</gll>, but only outputs to the side if the forward output is full.

This can be useful to prevent your factories from stalling, or prioritizing certain parts of your factory.
Entries.wiki.WKBelts_OverflowSplitter.text-robot: The overflow splitter was invented after the Cluster 4-D Incident. We lost 300,000 shapes that day. HR classified it as 'natural throughput reduction.'
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Display.title: Display
Entries.wiki.WKWires_Display.text-intro-1:
You can use a <gl>Display</gl> to display <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shapes</gll>, <gl>Numbers</gl> and <gl>Colors</gl>.

There are multiple sizes available, ranging from 1x1 to 3x3.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.title: Simulated Machines
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-intr: <gl>Simulated Machines</gl> perform <gl>Shape Operations</gl> on signals. The most common use is decomposing <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signals</gll> into individual <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Layers</gll> and <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Quarters</gll> as part of a <gll:WKWires_MaM>Make Anything Machine</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-rota: The <gl>Simulated Rotator</gl> and <gl>Simulated Reverse Rotator</gl> simulate rotating a <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signal</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-half: The Simulated Half Destroyer simulates destroying the Western Half of the Shape Signal.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-stac: The <gl>Simulated Stacker</gl> simulates <gll:WKProcessing_Stacker>Stacking</gll> two shape signals.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-unst: The Simulated Unstacker simulates separating the topmost Layer of the Shape Signal from the remainder. This does not break crystals.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-pain: The <gl>Simulated Painter</gl> simulates painting the <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signal</gll> with the <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluid</gll> from the <gl>Fluid Signal</gl> input.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-crys: The <gl>Simulated Crystal Generator</gl> simulates crystallizing the <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signal</gll> utilizing the <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluid</gll> from the <gl>Fluid Signal</gl> input.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-swap: The Simulated Swapper simulates swapping the Western Halves of both Shape Signals.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-pin-: The <gl>Simulated Pin Pusher</gl> simulates pushing up the <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Shape Signal</gll> with <gll:WKShapes_Pins>Support Pins</gll>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_VirtualProcessing.text-robo: They simulate real machines but process signals instead of shapes. R&D calls them 'virtual.' The power bill calls them 'very real.'
Entries.wiki.WKWires_ShapeAnalyzer.title: Shape Analyzer
Entries.wiki.WKWires_ShapeAnalyzer.text-intro-1: The Shape Analyzer allows you to analyze the North-East, first layer quarter of a Shape Signal, outputting both the Color and Base Shape of that quarter. This can be useful to deconstruct shapes as part of a dynamic factory, often called Make Anything Machine.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WireCrossings.title: Wire Crossings
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WireCrossings.text-intro-1: There are two types of intersections when working with <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Wires</gll>. You can either join wires using a <gl>Wire Cross</gl> or place a <gl>Wire Bridge</gl> to cross two wires without them intersecting.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WireTransmission.title: Wire Transmission
Entries.wiki.WKWires_WireTransmission.text-intro:
Use <gl>Wire Transmitters</gl> and <gl>Wire Receivers</gl> to transmit signals over short distances or between <gl>Platforms</gl>.

Since they only have a short range, you have to use <gll:WKWires_GlobalTransmission>Global Transmitters</gll> if you want to connect platforms across long distances.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_GlobalTransmission.title: Global Transmitters
Entries.wiki.WKWires_GlobalTransmission.text-int: You can use <gl>Global Signal Transmitters</gl> and <gl>Global Signal Receivers</gl> to transport <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Signals</gll> over large distances.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_GlobalTransmission.text-tra: The <gl>Global Signal Transmitter</gl> accepts an <gl>Input Signal</gl> and a <gl>Channel Signal</gl> and broadcasts the input signal on the specified channel. The <gl>Channel Signal</gl> can either be a <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Shape</gll>, <gll:WKFluids_Intro>Fluid</gll> or a <gl>Number</gl>.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_GlobalTransmission.text-rob: Think of the transmitter as a signal broadcaster - "I'm sending <icon:CuCuCuCu/> on a frequency of 523 MHz."
Entries.wiki.WKWires_GlobalTransmission.text-rec: The <gl>Global Signal Receiver</gl> accepts a <gl>Channel Signal</gl> and outputs any signal that is sent to that channel.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.title: Make Anything Machine (MaM)
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-intro-1: A dynamic factory that can produce any shape is called a Make Anything Machine. Once you unlock Random Operator Goals, building a new factory per shape is no longer practical. The goal: a single factory that dynamically adjusts its production using Wires. This is a complex challenge - start small and work your way up. Here are some hints to get you started.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-receive: You can unlock the <gl>Operator Shape Receiver</gl> in the <gll:WKResearch_Shop>Shop</gll>, which can be configured to output the currently requested shapes as a logical <gll:WKWires_WiresSignals>Signal</gll>. This can serve as the starting point for configuring the factory.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-analyze: By utilizing <gll:WKWires_ShapeAnalyzer>Shape Analyzers</gll> and <gll:WKWires_VirtualProcessing>Simulated Rotators</gll>, you can analyze every <gll:WKShapes_Shapes>Quarter</gll> of the requested shape individually, so you can find out what your factory needs to produce in the first place.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-selectshape: Use <gll:WKWires_BeltFilter>Belt Filters</gll> in combination with the results from the previous hint to dynamically select which quarters you want to stack together.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-paint: Combining the results from hint 2 with the <gll:WKWires_PipeGate>Pipe Gate</gll> allows you to dynamically paint the requested shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-stacking: To combine the shape again, since quarters can be empty, you will need to be smart about stacking them only if needed:
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-tip-combined:
Combine the dynamically painted and selected shapes from all the hints to build your first dynamic factory.

Consider this as a starting point - random shapes at higher operator level will require multiple Shape Layers, Pins, Crystals and more.
Entries.wiki.WKWires_MaM.text-robot: If you pull this off, you've basically beaten the game. The rest is just scaling. No big deal.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Handbook.title: Employee Handbook
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Handbook.text-body: Welcome to Vortex LTD. This handbook has been provided to all new Operators as part of the onboarding process. Please read it carefully. Section 1: Company Mission Vortex LTD exists to collect, process, and deliver shapes to the Vortex. The reasons for this are not your concern. Section 2: Workplace Conduct Operators are expected to maintain their factories in an orderly fashion. Unsanctioned communication with the Vortex is strictly prohibited. Not that it would respond. Section 3: Liability For full details, please refer to the Legal Agreement presented during your onboarding. You did read it, right? By continuing your assignment, you confirm acceptance of all terms - including the ones you probably skipped.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Handbook.text-robot: The handbook was last updated before the company was founded. I flagged this. They said it was intentional.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Pigment.title: Pigment Memo
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Pigment.text-body: INTERNAL MEMO - FLUID RESOURCES DIVISION RE: Pigment sourcing for paint production Following repeated Operator inquiries, this memo serves to clarify the origin of colored Fluids used in painting operations. The fluid deposits found on platforms are a natural byproduct of the spatial environment. They have always been there. There is no manufacturing process that creates them. They simply exist. We have been asked why the fluids match our exact color requirements. The official answer is coincidence. Please do not submit further inquiries on this topic. The Pigment Research Team has been dissolved as of last quarter. Their findings have been archived.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Pigment.text-robot: The fluids were here before the platforms. The platforms were here before us. I try not to think about what that means.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_RailIncident.title: Bulletin #4091
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_RailIncident.text-body: VORTEX LTD - SAFETY BULLETIN #4091 Incident: Unauthorized rail network expansion Location: Platform Cluster 7-G Severity: Under review On the date in question, Train routes in Cluster 7-G were observed to extend beyond the boundaries of the assigned platform network. Specifically, rail segments appeared on platforms that had not yet been placed by any Operator. The locomotives continued their routes without interruption. The cargo they carried upon return did not match any known shape configuration. Corrective action: The affected rail segments have been removed. Operators are reminded that trains should only run on tracks you have built. If you see tracks you didn't build, report them immediately and do not - under any circumstances - board the train.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_RailIncident.text-robot: Bulletin #4092 was about the same incident. It was issued before #4091. Nobody knows why.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Stilts.title: Project STILTS
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Stilts.text-body: R&D LAB NOTES - PROJECT STILTS Classification: Internal Project objective: Develop a mechanism to insert structural Pins into shape layers without compromising shape integrity. Phase 1 notes: Initial prototypes worked as expected. The pins could be pushed into shapes mechanically, providing structural support for tall configurations. Phase 2 notes: During stress testing, we discovered that shapes with pins in specific configurations would resonate at frequencies outside our measurement range. The lab equipment couldn't detect it - but the researchers could feel it. Phase 3 notes: Project STILTS has been approved for full deployment. The resonance issue has been classified as a feature. Management says it's fine. All lab staff have been reassigned. Their desks were already cleared when we arrived this morning.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Stilts.text-robot: The pins don't just hold shapes together. The shapes hold the pins together. I verified this twice and I still don't like it.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_NinthColor.title: The Ninth Color
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_NinthColor.text-body: CHROMATIC RESEARCH DIVISION - FINAL REPORT Status: DECOMMISSIONED The following is the final report from the Color Mixing research initiative. As established, the eight standard Colors can be combined to produce the full operational spectrum. Red, green, blue, yellow, cyan, magenta, white, and the absence of color. However, during extended experimentation, the research team reported observing a ninth chromatic output when mixing under very specific - and classified - conditions. Instruments could not register the color. Photographic equipment failed to capture it. But all seventeen researchers described it identically. We are not publishing their description. The Chromatic Research Division has been permanently closed. All samples have been disposed of. If you encounter a color you cannot name, close your eyes and file a report.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_NinthColor.text-robot: I can process exactly eight colors. I have been told this is all that exist. I have no reason to doubt this. None whatsoever.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_VerticalClearance.title: Vertical Clearance
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_VerticalClearance.text-: INTERNAL MEMO - PLATFORM ENGINEERING RE: Authorization of third machine level Effective immediately, all Operators are cleared to construct machines on a third vertical level. This expansion was not originally planned. The two-level limit was established for structural and - we were told - 'perceptual safety' reasons. However, recent developments have required us to revise this policy. The official reason is increased throughput demand. The unofficial reason is that we need to build higher. Platform Engineering cannot disclose why downward expansion was rejected, but we want to assure all Operators that there is nothing below the platforms that warrants investigation. The sounds reported by third-shift crews are consistent with normal spatial settling. Please stop filing requests to build downward.
The platforms float. What they float on is above my clearance level. Literally.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CrystalBrief.title: Crystal Brief
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CrystalBrief.text-body: MATERIALS SCIENCE BRIEF - CRYSTAL COMPOUNDS For distribution to: All Operators with Crystal clearance Crystals are a unique material generated through the interaction of specific fluids under controlled conditions. They possess structural properties that exceed any conventional material in the Vortex LTD catalog. Key observations: - Crystal growth does not follow any known nucleation pattern - When observed under magnification, internal structures appear to contain geometry that should not exist at that scale - Crystal samples removed from the production environment continue to grow at approximately 0.3% per day, regardless of ambient conditions Engineering has approved crystals for use in all standard shape configurations. The continued growth has been classified as 'within acceptable tolerances.' Do not store crystals near completed shapes overnight. We cannot explain what happens, but the shapes are different in the morning.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CrystalBrief.text-robot: The crystal generator has no moving parts. I opened one to check. It was empty inside. It still works.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CompletionNotice.title: Completion Notice
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CompletionNotice.text-b: VORTEX LTD - OPERATOR COMPLETION NOTICE Congratulations, Operator. You have successfully demonstrated competence across all assessed disciplines. Your production metrics are within acceptable parameters. Your factory designs have been reviewed and filed. As of this notice, you are granted Full Operational Clearance. This entitles you to continued access to all Vortex LTD resources, technologies, and platforms. You may have noticed that there is no mention of an end date. That is because there isn't one. Your contract with Vortex LTD does not expire. We trust you will continue to deliver shapes to the Vortex with the same dedication you've shown so far. The Vortex appreciates your contributions - in whatever way a spatial anomaly can appreciate things. Please do not attempt to leave the simulation. There's no button for that anyway.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_CompletionNotice.text-r: I've processed thousands of these notices. Not once has anyone asked what 'Full Operational Clearance' actually clears you for.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Anomaly221.title: Anomaly #221
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Anomaly221.text-body: ANOMALY REPORT #221 - VORTEX MONITORING STATION Classification: Restricted On the date in question, the Vortex deviated from established behavioral parameters. Standard Vortex behavior: Receives shapes. Processes them. Remains stable. Observed behavior: The Vortex produced a shape. A single, fully formed shape emerged from the Vortex and was deposited on the nearest conveyor belt. The shape matched no known configuration. When analyzed, its shape code returned a string that was longer than our systems could parse. The shape was quarantined and subsequently vanished from containment. The Vortex has since returned to normal operation. Monitoring staff have been reminded that the Vortex is a one-way system. It receives shapes. It does not send them. What happened on this date did not happen. Further observation of the Vortex at close range has been suspended indefinitely.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Anomaly221.text-robot: I ran the shape code through every validator we have. They all returned 'valid.' That's the part that worries me.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_BoardMinutes.title: Board Minutes
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_BoardMinutes.text-body: VORTEX LTD - BOARD MEETING MINUTES Attendees: [REDACTED] Date: [REDACTED] Agenda Item 1: Operational Expansion The board approved continued recruitment of new Operators. Current throughput is [REDACTED] shapes per cycle, which represents [REDACTED]% of the target. The target was not disclosed to the minutes-taker. Agenda Item 2: The Question of Purpose A board member raised the question of what the Vortex does with the shapes it receives. The question was tabled. It has been tabled at every meeting for the past [REDACTED] years. Agenda Item 3: Employee Awareness The board discussed whether Operators should be informed about [REDACTED]. The motion was defeated 7-1. The dissenting member's name has been removed from company records. These minutes have been approved for archival. They have not been approved for reading.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_BoardMinutes.text-robot: The meeting was scheduled for two hours. The audio recording is eleven seconds long.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_VasquezLog.title: Dr. Vornath's Log
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_VasquezLog.text-body: PERSONAL LOG - DR. KEL VORNATH Senior Researcher, Vortex Observation Division Day 1: Transferred to the Vortex Observation Division today. Exciting opportunity. The Vortex is magnificent up close - a perfect geometric spiral of impossible depth. I can see why they study it. Day 45: The instruments say the Vortex is 12 meters across. When I look at it, I know that's wrong. It's much, much larger than that. The instruments aren't lying - they just can't measure what it actually is. Day 102: I've started dreaming in shape codes. I wake up with configurations I've never seen. When I build them in the simulation, they work. They produce outputs that shouldn't be possible. I haven't reported this yet. Day 187: I understand now. The shapes aren't products. They're cross-sections. Every shape we build is a shadow of something that exists in more dimensions than the simulation allows. We're assembling a structure we can't perceive - not because it's hidden, but because we don't have enough dimensions to see it. Day 188: I can see it now. [No further entries]
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_VasquezLog.text-robot: Dr. Vornath's employee record shows they never existed. Their log was found in a filing cabinet that also never existed.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Singularity.title: [LOCATION UNKNOWN]
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Singularity.text-body: [DOCUMENT RECOVERED FROM UNKNOWN LOCATION] [AUTHOR: UNKNOWN] [DATE: BEFORE] The Operators believe they are building factories. This is correct. The Operators believe they are delivering shapes. This is correct. The Operators believe the shapes are consumed. This is almost correct. The shapes are not consumed. They are remembered. Every shape delivered to the Vortex becomes part of a structure that exists outside the simulation. You cannot see it from inside. You were never meant to. The Vortex is not a machine. It is not an anomaly. It is a door. Every shape is a tumbler. When the last shape clicks into place, the door opens. We do not know what is on the other side. We know that it has been waiting. We know that it is patient. We know that it taught us how to build the Vortex in the first place. Keep building, Operator. You're doing so well.
Entries.wiki.WKVortexLTD_Singularity.text-robot: I was not programmed to feel fear. I want to make that very clear. This is informational, not emotional.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_MapGeneration.title: Map Generation
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_MapGeneration.text-intro-1:
Before starting a savegame, you can customize <gl>Map Generation</gl> parameters.

Change the frequency of <gl>Shape Asteroids</gl>, allow mixed fluids on the map, or enable a <gl>Spiral</gl> pattern for asteroids - great for making a second playthrough more interesting.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Challenges.title: Challenges
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Challenges.text-intro-1: When creating a savegame, you can enable Challenges for extra difficulty - like Only 1x1 Platforms or No Straight Stacker. Completing challenges grants Operator Bragging Rights.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Challenges.text-robot: Some Operators found the game too easy. So we made it harder. They still complained. We respect that.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Difficulty.title: Difficulty
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Difficulty.text-intro-1: Change difficulty at any time via the Gear icon on your savegame in the Main Menu. Multiple Difficulty Presets are available. You can also fine-tune Platform Capacity Multiplier, Research Shape Cost Multiplier, and Blueprint Cost. Relaxed: Unlimited platforms, reduced shape requirements. Challenge: Blueprints cost Blueprint Credits earned by delivering specific shapes.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_ConsoleCommands.title: Console Commands
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_ConsoleCommands.text-intro-1: You can use <gl>F1</gl> to open the <gl>Debug Console</gl>. Use the <gl>help</gl> command to view a list of all commands.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_ConsoleCommands.text-no-chea: calc [expression] - Allows you to calculate any expression, for example calc (5+3)*3/2 camera.set-movement-smoothing [float] - Makes the camera move smoother, useful for capturing videos. camera.disable-restrictions-danger - Allows you to tilt your camera beyond the limits, often causing performance issues though. debug.show-charts - Toggles an FPS / Memory overlay. savegames.open-folder - Opens the savegames folder on your disk. logs.open-folder - Opens the logs folder, containing the Player.log file.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_ConsoleCommands.text-cheats: Run cheats.enable first. This permanently flags your savegame with a warning in the Operator Level tab. god - Sandbox mode: unlocks all research, producers, credits and more. hub.set [shape] [amount] - Sets shapes stored in the Vortex. research.set-points [amount] - Sets your Research Credits.
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Feedback.title: Feedback & Bugs
Entries.wiki.WKMisc_Feedback.text-intro-1: We appreciate any feedback you have! Discord: Our preferred channel for community discussion and Bug Reports. Steam Forums: Our QA team checks these regularly. Suggestions Portal: Post and vote on suggestions, and see what's planned. E-Mail: Reach us at support@tobspr.io and our QA team will respond.
Entries.tutorial.TSSkillAcademy_OpenWiki: Open the Codex in the top right.
Entries.tutorial.TSSkillAcademy_BeltAnchor: Press while dragging a Belt to place an Anchor that locks the belt to a fixed point.
Entries.tutorial.TSSkillAcademy_FlipBlueprint: Flip a blueprint or structure with <hotkey:building-placement.mirror/> or <hotkey:building-placement.mirror-inverse/>.
Entries.tutorial.TPConditionalPopups_AreaDeleteP: You can quickly delete an entire Area by dragging with + . Very satisfying. Not that I'd know.
Entries.tutorial.TPConditionalPopups_VortexRotat: The Vortex accepts shapes in any orientation, so you don't need to rotate them before delivering them. I mean ... have you tried rotating your camera?
Entries.tutorial.TSCinematricIntro.step-intro: Alright - we've set up an individual simulation for you. Can't have you mess up our real factories. <info>Not yet, anyway.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSCinematricIntro.step-vortex-p: This is your <gl>Vortex Platform</gl>. It's not much, but you'll unlock more space as you progress. <info>Plenty for now, right?</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSCinematricIntro.step-vortex:
See that thing in the center? That's your <gl>Vortex</gl>. Deliver <gl>Shapes</gl> to it to complete your certification.

<info>Ever wonder where those shapes end up? Manufacture Mode has the answers.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSCinematricIntro.step-asteroid:
We've scattered a few <gl>Shape Asteroids</gl> around the platform.

<info>Due to recent budget cuts, automatic extraction has been discontinued. You'll need to extract them yourself.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSCinematricIntro.step-final: One last tip: always make sure to [ERROR: SECTION MISSING] and you'll be just fine. That's everything! Good luck, Operator.
Entries.tutorial.TSConverterCinematricIntro.step: Oh, you again. Remember that little incident with your <gl>Vortex</gl>? Yeah - we're fixing that today.
This is your <gl>Dimensional Waste Receiver</gl>. All that dimensional waste has to go somewhere - and that somewhere is your problem now.
Process the waste at <gl>Polishing Stations</gl>, then trade the results at <gl>Trade Stations</gl> for <gl>Refined Shapes</gl>.

Rebuild your <gl>Vortex Platform</gl> and we'll consider giving you a real simulation again.

<info>No pressure.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSConverter_BuildRecyclers: Build a <gl>Polishing Station</gl> to start processing the waste.
Entries.tutorial.TSConverter_DeliverFirstShape: Take the waste from the <gl>Dimensional Waste Receiver</gl>, cut it, and deliver it to the <gl>Polishing Station</gl>.
Entries.tutorial.TSConverter_ScaleUp: Once you complete the Milestone, the station will output Refined Shapes in return. Your current factory is way too slow, though. Scale up to at least 8 Polishing Stations.
Entries.tutorial.TSConverterSecondStation_BuildS:
Not bad, Operator.

Build a <gl>Ruby Station</gl> and supply it to start gathering <gl>Refined Shapes</gl>.
Entries.tutorial.TSConverterResearch_BuildStatio:
Build a <gl>Donation Station</gl> to start generating <gl><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gl>.

<info>Vortex LTD appreciates your generous contributions.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSExtractorBelts_3_ConnectExtra: Place Belts on the highlighted areas to connect the Mini-Miners to the Vortex on the right.
Entries.tutorial.TSExtractorBelts_4_AwaitCircleD:
<info>I guess we're doing circles now:</info>

Deliver <icon:CuCuCuCu/> <progress0/>
Entries.tutorial.TPRotationHintPopup.title: Oops - Wrong Direction!
Entries.tutorial.TSHalfDestroyers_2_ConnectHalfD: Split the belt to supply all Half Destroyers.
Entries.tutorial.TPSplittersPopup.title: Splitting Belts
Entries.tutorial.TPSplittersPopup.description: Drag Conveyor Belts to split them. Conveyor Belts are faster than machines, so use one belt to supply multiple machines. One belt, many machines. That's the motto around here.
Entries.tutorial.TPHalfDestroyersPopup.title: Combining Machines
Entries.tutorial.TPHalfDestroyersPopup.descripti:
The <gl>Half Destroyer</gl> destroys the <gl>Western</gl> half of a shape.

Combine it with the <gl>Rotator</gl> to choose which side gets destroyed.

Use <gl>Half Destroyer</gl> <icon:ArrowRight/> <gl>Rotator</gl> <icon:ArrowRight/> <gl>Half Destroyer</gl> to create quarters <icon:Cu------/>.

<info>We've simulated a magnetic North in space by linking it to the Boomerang Nebula. Do not attempt to reach the Nebula.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSFirstMilestoneDelivery_1_Comp: Well done, Operator. Connect the Half Destroyers and deliver the remaining shapes, displayed below. PS: My sensors detected some Squares southeast.
Entries.tutorial.TPMergersPopup.title: Merging Belts
Entries.tutorial.TPMergersPopup.description: Drag Conveyor Belts to merge them. Machines are slower than Conveyor Belts, so always merge the output of multiple machines onto one belt. The reverse of splitting. Shocking, I know.
Entries.tutorial.TSStacking_2_ConnectStackerInpu: The Stacker has two inputs, one at the bottom and one at the top. Deliver to the upper input and to the lower input to stack them.
Entries.tutorial.TSStacking_3_ConnectStackerOutp: Connect the output of the <gl>Stacker</gl> to the <gl>Vortex</gl>.
Entries.tutorial.TPStacking_StackerArrayTutorial: Stackers are slow - you need 6 of them to keep up with a single conveyor belt. Place them in an array, though more creative layouts are absolutely possible:
Entries.tutorial.TSStackingAdvance_4_MoreStacker: Stackers are really slow. Place more of them to keep up with demand. We don't have all day here.
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltLiftTutorial.title: 2nd Machine Level
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltLiftTutorial.description: You've unlocked a second machine level. This lets you place conveyors and machines on multiple levels - which the Stacker requires. Practice placing Belt Lifts here until you get the hang of it. This one trips up most new Operators. Take your time.
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltLiftTutorial.step0-moveMo: Move your cursor into the circle
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltLiftTutorial.step3-moveRi: Keep holding and move your cursor into the circle
Entries.tutorial.TPGenericTutorialDialog.dialog-: Vortex LTD requires all Operators to complete mandatory training. The mechanics covered here are essential for what's ahead. Are you sure?
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.title: Conveyor Belts
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.descripti: Conveyor Belts transport Shapes from A to B. Anchors lock the belt to a fixed point, making placement much easier. Practice placing Conveyor Belts and Anchors here:
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.step0-mov: Move your cursor into the circle
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.step3-mov: Keep holding and move your cursor into the circle
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.step4-pla: Keep holding and press to place an Anchor .
Entries.tutorial.TPBeltAnchorsTutorial.step6-pla: Keep holding and press to place another Anchor .
Entries.tutorial.TSResearch_1_OpenTasks: Got a deal for you. Open the Jobs Screen.
Entries.tutorial.TSResearch_2_GatherResearchPoin:
Deliver the listed shapes to the <gl>Vortex</gl>.

<info>You'll want those <gl><icon:ResearchPoints/> Research Credits</gl>. Trust me.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TSResearch_3_SpendResearchPoint: Good job. Now treat yourself to something from the <gl>Shop</gl> - you earned it.
Entries.tutorial.TSBlueprints_1_PlaceBlueprint: Select an Area with and press to create a Blueprint. Place the blueprint with .
Entries.tutorial.TPBlueprints.title: Blueprints - Copy & Paste
Entries.tutorial.TPBlueprints.description:
Built something nice? Reuse it with <gl>Blueprints</gl>.

Select an <gl>Area</gl> with <hotkey:mass-selection.select-area-modifier/> <hotkey:mass-selection.select-base/>, then press <hotkey:mass-selection.make-blueprint/> to <gl>Copy</gl> it.

This creates a <gl>Blueprint</gl> you can place with <hotkey:building-placement.confirm-placement/>.

<info>Build once, paste forever. The lazy Operator's best friend.</info>
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_Foundations.title: Machine Platforms
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_Foundations.descripti: Place Machine Platforms to get more space for your Machines. Your Platform Capacity (upper left) determines how many you can place. Tip: build them as big, reusable modules - for example, a dedicated Rotator or Stacker platform you can blueprint and reuse. We provide the floor. The rest is your problem.
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_MinerExtensions.title: Miner Boosters
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_MinerExtensions.descr: Connect 3 Miner Boosters to an Asteroid Miner to unlock additional Extraction Spots on the platform. R&D tested every number from one to five. Three won.
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_SpaceBelts.title: Space Belts
Entries.tutorial.TPIslands_SpaceBelts.descriptio: Use Space Belts to connect platforms. They work like regular belts, with support for Anchors and lifts across multiple levels. Each space belt has 4 Lanes per machine level. Every lane carries 4 conveyor belts worth of throughput, so you can merge up to 4 machine platforms into one space belt. Shapes stay in their lane - input on the lower-right comes out on the lower-right. If only Operators stayed in their lane, too.
Entries.tutorial.TSIslands_1_PlaceMiner: Enter Space View by zooming out . Place an Asteroid Miner on the green star asteroid to the northeast. Mining rights included. You're welcome.
Entries.tutorial.TSIslands_2_PlaceExtensions: Place 3 Miner Boosters to unlock the additional Extraction Spots on the miner.
Entries.tutorial.TSIslands_3_SpaceBelts: Use Space Belts to connect the Asteroid Miner to the Vortex.
Entries.tutorial.TSIslands_4_PlacePlatform:
Solid work, Operator.

Place a <gl>Machine Platform</gl> to expand your factory.
